res.summary: The app has 62 page.tsx routes organized into roughly five zones: a marketing/auth public set at app root, a public "trust" set (/models, /track-record, /regime, /markets), an authenticated platform set, an admin console, and onboarding. The shell architecture is INCONSISTENT and fragmented: only routes inside the (platform)/ route group inherit the canonical AppShell (Sidebar + Topbar + CommandPalette + MobileDrawer) from a layout.tsx. A large number of authenticated pages live OUTSIDE (platform)/ at app root (signals, portfolio, watchlist, trades, stocks, markets, settings, stock/[symbol]) and must each import <AppShell> manually inside their own page.tsx — there is no shared layout for them, so the shell is re-mounted per navigation and easy to forget. Three further navigation patterns coexist: AppShell (app), a bespoke inline mini-navbar hand-rolled in each public page (/regime, /models, /track-record), LightNavbar+Footer (landing/pricing), and a fully custom terminal header on /stock/[symbol] with no sidebar at all. The header-less-desktop intent IS implemented (Topbar is lg:hidden; Sidebar carries everything). Dual-mode Managed/Pro is wired through a single chokepoint (UiModeContext → NavList swaps NAV for MANAGED_NAV), which is clean. Route gating is layered (middleware cookie check + ClientAuthGate) but has known dev bypasses and is auth-only — there is NO route-level tier gating (tier is enforced only by in-page upgrade CTAs). There is a real routing CONFLICT: /fo-strategies is simultaneously a live page and a middleware 301-redirect target, making it unreachable by URL. The CommandPalette nav list is stale (lists "Copilot" not "Main Chat", and points at engine slugs/labels that drifted from nav.ts). Two competing authenticated "home" surfaces exist (/copilot Main Chat vs /dashboard Command Center).

inventory.name: middleware.ts (route gate + CSP + retired-route redirects)
inventory.notes: 301-redirects 26 retired v1 routes; publicPaths set + PUBLIC_PREFIXES (/models,/track-record,/regime,/markets); cookie-presence auth check (sb-*-auth-token); DEV bypass when no session cookie; injects per-request CSP nonce. Matcher excludes _next/static, _next/image, favicon.

inventory.name: AppShell.tsx
inventory.notes: Three-zone shell: Sidebar (desktop) + Topbar (mobile) + main. Owns paletteOpen/drawerOpen/collapsed state, Cmd/Ctrl+K palette toggle, ESC close, localStorage 'quantx.sidebar.collapsed.v2'. Ambient aurora + skip-to-content link.

inventory.name: Sidebar.tsx
inventory.notes: Header-less collapsible glass rail (w-60 / w-68px). Brand→/copilot, New-chat→/copilot, Workspace NavList, date-grouped Recent Chats (SWR copilot:conversations, delete on hover), pinned footer (Notifications→/inbox, Settings→/settings), social strip (all href='#' placeholders), collapse toggle at bottom.

inventory.name: Topbar.tsx
inventory.notes: Mobile-only (lg:hidden) bar: hamburger + brand→/copilot. Confirms header-less-desktop intent.

inventory.name: NavList.tsx
inventory.notes: Shared nav renderer for Sidebar + MobileDrawer. Reads useUiMode(): managed→MANAGED_NAV (flat 4 items, no headers), pro→NAV grouped by section. Single mode chokepoint. Renders Pro/Elite tier badges (cosmetic only).

inventory.name: nav.ts
inventory.notes: NAV = 14 items in workspace/ai/account sections; MANAGED_NAV = 4 (Home/My Money/Activity/Settings). Comment notes /trades,/engines,/paper-trading,/referrals are sidebar-excluded, palette-only. F&O tier:'elite', AutoPilot tier:'pro'.

inventory.name: CommandPalette.tsx
inventory.notes: Cmd+K palette with hardcoded ENTRIES list. STALE/DRIFTED: labels '/copilot'→'Copilot' (sidebar says 'Main Chat'), missing /markets,/fno,/risk; includes engine slugs; GROUP_ORDER has dead 'Agents' group. Placeholder claims symbol/strategy search but only filters static nav labels.

inventory.name: MobileDrawer.tsx
inventory.notes: lg:hidden slide-over: brand, New chat, Workspace NavList (tall tap targets), footer Notifications/Settings + social strip. Focus-traps close button. Same social href='#' placeholders.

inventory.name: AutopilotStickyStop.tsx
inventory.notes: Mobile-only (md:hidden) floating pause FAB, renders only when api.autoTrader.status().enabled. One-tap pause modal. Mounted in (platform)/layout — so it does NOT appear on the app-root authed pages (signals/portfolio/etc.) that import AppShell directly.

inventory.name: appFont.ts
inventory.notes: Inter (sans) + JetBrains Mono — scoped to AppShell root only; marketing/auth keep root DM Sans/DM Mono. Applied via CSS vars on AppShell div.

inventory.name: UiModeContext.tsx

inventory.name: AuthContext.tsx
inventory.notes: Supabase auth; bridges session→cookie (sb-<ref>-auth-token=1) so cookie-only middleware passes. signIn→/dashboard (with MFA AAL2 gate→/login/mfa, but /login/mfa is a RETIRED route→/settings?tab=security). signUp via backend /api/auth/signup then auto sign-in.

inventory.name: ClientAuthGate.tsx
inventory.notes: Defence-in-depth client redirect to /login for PROTECTED_PREFIXES (17 prefixes). Has NEXT_PUBLIC_DISABLE_AUTH dev bypass (double-guarded). 600ms NAV_GRACE_MS to avoid post-login bounce loop. Includes stale '/assistant' prefix (retired route).

inventory.name: (platform)/layout.tsx
inventory.notes: Wraps children in CopilotProvider+SystemHaltBanner+CopilotQuotaModal+AppShell+AutopilotStickyStop. First-login redirect→/onboarding/risk-quiz via onboardingStatusCache; referral attribution; Web Push subscribe. Only applies to (platform)/ routes.

inventory.name: app/layout.tsx (root)
inventory.notes: html lang=en class='light'; DM Sans/DM Mono; metadata/OG; ThemeProvider defaultTheme='light' enableSystem storageKey='quantx.theme'. NOTE title still 'AI Swing Trading Platform' (codename leak risk: 'Swing' is internal-only).

inventory.name: providers.tsx
inventory.notes: ThemeProvider + SWRConfig + AuthProvider + UiModeProvider + ClientAuthGate + ThemedToaster + card-spotlight pointermove. Theme toggle nowhere in providers — only in Settings (matches intent).

inventory.name: admin/layout.tsx
inventory.notes: Separate fixed-264px sidebar (warning/amber accent) — does NOT reuse AppShell. 7 nav items. Server-verified admin gate (/api/admin/verify) with Access Denied fallback. Uses legacy d-bg/d-border/text-white tokens, not the AI Trading OS palette.

inventory.name: Landing page
inventory.notes: LightNavbar + 7 sections (Hero/TrackRecord/FeatureGrid F1-F12/HowItWorks/PricingPreview/Footer). Redirects signed-in users: managed→/home, pro→/copilot. Copy says 'Five AI engines — Forecast·Alpha·Gate·Regime·Mood' which DRIFTS from lib/engines.ts (Alpha·Mood·Regime·AutoPilot) and brand-firewall.

inventory.name: Public trust pages
inventory.notes: Public per middleware PUBLIC_PREFIXES. NO AppShell and NO LightNavbar — each hand-rolls its own inline mini-navbar (Quant X / Track record / Models / Pricing / Start free). Third nav pattern; duplicated 3x; inconsistent with both app and landing.

inventory.name: /markets
inventory.notes: Public per middleware BUT wraps itself in full AppShell — so a logged-out visitor sees the authenticated app sidebar (New chat, Workspace nav, Recent Chats SWR which 401s and is swallowed). IA mismatch: public data page rendered inside the private shell.

inventory.name: /stock/[symbol]
inventory.notes: Fully custom full-screen terminal: own top header with router.back(), NO sidebar, NO AppShell. Fourth distinct shell pattern. Live WS price ticks.

inventory.name: App-root authed pages (no shared layout)
inventory.notes: Each manually imports <AppShell>. signals/[id], trades/[id], portfolio/doctor, settings also do. CategorySignalsPage (signals/{momentum,swing,positional,intraday}) wraps AppShell internally. Means AppShell unmount/remount on cross-section nav vs (platform) routes that keep it via layout.

inventory.name: (platform) routes
inventory.notes: 20 routes get AppShell via layout: dashboard, copilot, autopilot(+track-record), engines(+[slug]), fno, fo-strategies, home, activity, risk, scanner, strategies(+[slug],/deployed,/mine/[id]), inbox(+[id]), paper-trading, referrals, error/loading.

inventory.name: Onboarding flow
inventory.notes: 3-step: risk-quiz (5Q→profile+tier rec), broker-connect (Zerodha/Upstox/Angel), complete (4 CTA cards→dashboard/copilot/paper-trading/strategies). No onboarding/layout.tsx — each page is standalone full-screen. (platform)/layout forces incomplete users to /onboarding/risk-quiz.

inventory.name: Auth/broker callbacks
inventory.notes: auth/callback→/copilot on session. broker/callback handles Zerodha(sessionStorage state)/Upstox(URL state)/Angel(credential, bounces to /settings). Both public per middleware.

inventory.name: lib/hooks/useTier.ts
inventory.notes: SWR-cached /api/user/tier → tier/isAdmin/features/copilotDailyCap + predicates. Used for in-page upgrade CTAs ONLY — not wired to any route guard.

inventory.name: lib/engines.ts
inventory.notes: Brand-firewall public engine list: Alpha·Mood·Regime·AutoPilot (4, all 'production'). No raw model names. Counterpoint NOT here despite being a 'public engine' per design intent. /engines page header still says '8 ML/DL engines' (stale comment) while rendering 4.

inventory.name: settings/_components/ModePanel.tsx
inventory.notes: Managed/Pro radio switch; on change setMode() + router.push(managed?'/home':'/copilot'). The only UI to change mode (matches 'mode switch in Settings' intent).

strengths: Header-less desktop is genuinely implemented: Topbar is lg:hidden, Sidebar carries brand/nav/recent-chats/utility-footer/collapse-toggle exactly as the IA intent specifies.

Dual-mode (Managed vs Pro) is architected through a single clean chokepoint: UiModeContext drives NavList, so both Sidebar and MobileDrawer swap to the 4-item MANAGED_NAV with zero per-component mode wiring. Mode persists per-account (ui_preferences) with a localStorage mirror for flicker-free paint.
NavList is a true single source of truth for active-state + styling, shared by desktop sidebar and mobile drawer — no divergence between the two.
Recent-chats sidebar is a polished ChatGPT-style date-grouped list with optimistic delete + active highlighting, backed by real SWR data.

Defence-in-depth auth: middleware cookie gate + AuthContext session→cookie bridge + ClientAuthGate, with a documented (and double-guarded) dev bypass. The cookie-vs-localStorage race and post-login bounce loop are explicitly handled (NAV_GRACE_MS, INITIAL_SESSION cookie restore).
Retired-route redirect map preserves 26 old deep-links/bookmarks with 301s before the auth gate — good for SEO and notification links.
Accessibility basics are present: skip-to-content link, aria-current on active nav, role=listbox/option on the palette, focus management in MobileDrawer, aria-labels throughout.
Brand firewall holds in the engine surface (lib/engines.ts) — only public names, no TFT/Qlib/FinBERT/HMM leakage.

issues.title: Shell is applied two incompatible ways — (platform) layout vs per-page <AppShell> imports
issues.detail: Only the 20 routes inside app/(platform)/ inherit AppShell from a route-group layout.tsx. A large set of authenticated pages live at app root (signals, signals/[id], portfolio, portfolio/doctor, watchlist, trades, trades/[id], stocks, markets, settings) and each MANUALLY imports <AppShell> inside its own page.tsx (CategorySignalsPage wraps it internally for the momentum/swing/positional/intraday signal pages). Consequences: (1) AppShell unmounts and fully remounts when navigating between an app-root page and a (platform) page — sidebar state, scroll, Recent-Chats SWR all reset, causing visible jank; (2) AutopilotStickyStop + CopilotProvider + SystemHaltBanner (mounted only in (platform)/layout) are ABSENT on every app-root page — the global pause FAB and system-halt banner silently don't render on signals/portfolio/etc.; (3) any new app-root page that forgets the import renders with no shell. This is the single biggest structural debt for the redesign.

issues.title: /fo-strategies is both a live page and a 301-redirect target — page unreachable by URL
issues.detail: middleware.ts RETIRED_ROUTE_REDIRECTS maps '/fo-strategies' → '/strategies?filter=options' with a 301. But app/(platform)/fo-strategies/page.tsx is a fully live F&O strategies dashboard, and app/(platform)/fno/page.tsx links to /fo-strategies as its 'Strategy Lab' deep workspace. Middleware runs before routing, so any direct navigation or link to /fo-strategies 301s away and the live page is only reachable if the redirect is somehow bypassed. Same class of latent conflict for '/momentum'→'/strategies?filter=momentum' if a /momentum route is ever added. Net: a built feature is effectively dead via its own URL.

issues.title: CommandPalette nav list is stale and drifted from nav.ts
issues.detail: ENTRIES hardcodes its own list instead of deriving from NAV. It labels /copilot as 'Copilot' (sidebar+IA say 'Main Chat'), is MISSING /markets, /fno, and /risk (all live sidebar items), has a dead 'Agents' group in GROUP_ORDER with no entries, and lists engine slugs whose phrasing differs from lib/engines.ts. The input placeholder promises 'Search nav, symbols, strategies…' but it only substring-filters static labels — no symbol or strategy search exists. Two sources of truth for navigation guarantee ongoing drift.

issues.title: Two competing authenticated 'home' surfaces (/copilot vs /dashboard)
issues.detail: Both auth/callback and the landing redirect Pro users to /copilot ('Main Chat hub + marketing home, the default landing'), and the sidebar brand + New-chat + Topbar all point to /copilot. Yet /dashboard is 'Command Center — the trader's mission control, the first screen each morning' and is the first NAV item + the onboarding-complete primary CTA ('Go to Command Center'). Users are pulled toward two different homes by different surfaces; the IA does not clearly designate one as THE home.

issues.title: Four distinct navigation chrome patterns across the app
issues.detail: (1) AppShell sidebar (app), (2) LightNavbar+Footer (landing, pricing), (3) a hand-rolled inline mini-navbar duplicated in /regime, /models, /track-record (Quant X / Track record / Models / Pricing / Start free), (4) a custom full-screen terminal header with no sidebar on /stock/[symbol]. The public trust pages' inline navbar is copy-pasted 3x and diverges from LightNavbar. No shared 'public shell' component exists.

issues.title: /markets is a public route rendered inside the PRIVATE app shell
issues.detail: middleware lists /markets in PUBLIC_PREFIXES (crawlable, no auth), but app/markets/page.tsx wraps its content in <AppShell>. A logged-out visitor therefore sees the full authenticated sidebar — 'New chat', Workspace nav links into gated pages, and a Recent-Chats SWR call (copilotListConversations) that 401s and is silently swallowed. The shell is semantically wrong for an unauthenticated marketing/data surface.

issues.title: No route-level tier gating — tier is enforced only by in-page CTAs
issues.detail: Routes are gated by auth only (middleware + ClientAuthGate). useTier()/features power upgrade prompts inside pages, and nav.ts shows cosmetic Pro/Elite badges, but a Free user can navigate directly to /autopilot (Pro), /fno (Elite), /fo-strategies, etc.; whether they see real content vs an upsell is left to each page's own logic. There is no central feature-gate wrapper, so enforcement is inconsistent and easy to miss on new pages.

issues.title: Stale routes referenced after their own redirect/removal
issues.detail: AuthContext routes MFA step-up to '/login/mfa', which is a RETIRED route in middleware (→/settings?tab=security) — the MFA challenge page is gone, so MFA users would be 301'd to settings instead of a challenge. ClientAuthGate PROTECTED_PREFIXES still lists '/assistant' (retired → /copilot). These dangling references are latent bugs.

issues.title: Orphan pages reachable only via Command Palette (or not at all)
issues.detail: Per nav.ts comment, /trades, /engines, /paper-trading, /referrals are intentionally excluded from the sidebar and 'reachable via the palette'. But /trades, /paper-trading, /referrals are NOT in the CommandPalette ENTRIES either (only /paper-trading, /trades, /referrals appear under Paper/Account groups — /engines is). /autopilot/track-record and /strategies/deployed, /strategies/mine/[id] have no nav entry anywhere. The public /track-record/[id] detail and /track-record list also have no in-app link. Effective discoverability is poor for ~8 live routes.

issues.title: Social links are dead placeholders (href='#') in both Sidebar and MobileDrawer
issues.detail: X/Telegram/Instagram/LinkedIn/YouTube all link to '#'. Duplicated in two components. Will render as visible-but-broken social row in production.

issues.title: Engine count + landing copy drift from the real 4-engine list
issues.detail: /engines page header comment says '8 ML/DL engines' but renders 4 from lib/engines.ts. Landing copy says 'Five AI engines — Forecast·Alpha·Gate·Regime·Mood' and '12 AI models', none of which match the canonical public set (Alpha·Mood·Regime·AutoPilot·Counterpoint). Counterpoint is a stated public engine but is absent from lib/engines.ts. Root <title> still says 'AI Swing Trading Platform' ('Swing' is internal codename per brand rules).

issues.title: admin console is a separate, un-restyled shell
issues.detail: admin/layout.tsx hand-rolls its own fixed sidebar with legacy tokens (d-bg, d-border, text-white, warning amber) and does not share AppShell or the AI Trading OS palette. It will be left behind by any token-swap redesign unless explicitly included.

redesign_implications: CONSOLIDATE THE SHELL FIRST. Move every authenticated app-root page (signals, portfolio, watchlist, trades, stocks, settings, stock/[symbol]) into the (platform)/ route group (or a shared (app)/ group) so AppShell, AutopilotStickyStop, CopilotProvider and SystemHaltBanner are applied ONCE via layout. This eliminates remount jank, fixes the missing global FAB/halt-banner on those pages, and removes the per-page <AppShell> import footgun. This is the highest-leverage structural change.

Introduce a dedicated PublicShell component for the public trust surfaces (/models, /track-record, /regime) and stop hand-rolling the inline navbar 3x; decide whether /markets is public (use PublicShell) or private (move under app group) — it currently straddles both.

Resolve the /fo-strategies redirect-vs-live-page conflict before redesign: either remove it from RETIRED_ROUTE_REDIRECTS (and keep the page) or fold the page into /strategies?filter=options and delete the page. As-is the redesign would be polishing an unreachable URL.

Pick ONE authenticated home (/copilot Main Chat OR /dashboard Command Center) and make every entry point (brand logo, New chat, auth callback, onboarding-complete CTA, landing redirect) agree. The current split is an IA decision the redesign must settle.

Drive CommandPalette + nav badges + any future feature-gate from a SINGLE nav manifest (extend nav.ts) so labels/tiers/visibility never drift; add real symbol/strategy search or fix the misleading placeholder.
If the product wants true tier gating (not just upsell CTAs), add a central FeatureGate/route-guard layer keyed off useTier(); today gating is page-by-page and inconsistent.

The Managed/Pro chokepoint (UiModeContext→NavList) is the pattern to KEEP and extend — the redesign should route mode-specific surfaces through this rather than re-introducing per-component mode checks.
Plan to restyle the admin console explicitly — it is on a separate legacy-token shell and a global token swap will not reach it.

Fix dangling route references (/login/mfa MFA path, /assistant in ClientAuthGate) and the brand/engine-count copy drift (landing 'Forecast·Gate·5 engines', /engines '8 engines', root title 'Swing') as part of the redesign content pass.
reusable_assets: NavList.tsx — the single shared active-state + tier-badge + mode-aware nav renderer (used by Sidebar + MobileDrawer). Keep and extend.
nav.ts — NAV / MANAGED_NAV / NAV_SECTIONS structure; the right place to make the canonical nav manifest the redesign derives everything from.
UiModeContext.tsx + ModePanel.tsx — the entire Managed/Pro dual-mode wiring (per-account persistence + localStorage mirror + read-merge-write). Reuse as-is.
AppShell.tsx three-zone layout + collapse persistence + Cmd+K + skip-to-content + ambient aurora — solid skeleton to re-skin rather than rebuild.
AuthContext.tsx session→cookie bridge + middleware cookie gate + ClientAuthGate grace-period pattern — the auth flow plumbing should be preserved (backend contract: Bearer JWT on every API call).
middleware.ts RETIRED_ROUTE_REDIRECTS map + CSP nonce strategy — keep the redirect equity and the working CSP (just reconcile the /fo-strategies entry).
lib/hooks/useTier.ts (SWR /api/user/tier) and lib/engines.ts (brand-firewall public engine list) — reuse for any gating/engine surfaces; engines.ts is the canonical brand-safe naming source.
AutopilotStickyStop.tsx — the mobile one-tap pause FAB; just needs to be mounted at the shared app layout so it appears on ALL authed pages, not only (platform) ones.
appFont.ts (Inter/JetBrains scoped to app) + providers.tsx ThemeProvider (light default, theme only in Settings) — token/theme infra to build the new palette on.

res.summary: The design system is a CSS-variable-driven token system ("Quant X v2 / AI Trading OS palette B") whose actual implemented signature color is a TradingView/LuxAlgo teal-green (#2BD9BC dark / #08A085 light), NOT the emerald-teal #00E6A7 from the redesign-intent brief — that intent palette is essentially unimplemented (it appears only twice, as a color-mix() fallback). Theming uses an inverted single-source strategy: :root in globals.css IS the dark theme, and html.light overrides surfaces/text to a "cool off-white" light theme. next-themes is wired with attribute="class", defaultTheme="light", storageKey="quantx.theme"; there are ZERO Tailwind dark: variants and NO html.dark selector, so darkMode:'class' in tailwind.config is effectively dead config — re-theming happens purely through ~25 CSS variables, which is good news for a redesign. The token layer is reasonably well adopted (text-primary 1145×, text-d-text-primary 767×, border tokens 692×, bg-wrap 313×), and arbitrary bg-[#hex] Tailwind classes are contained (25 occurrences, guarded by scripts/check_frontend_hex_literals.sh). The big liabilities: globals.css is a 2,528-line accumulation of overlapping eras (deep-space-neon → LuxAlgo glass → flat trading surfaces → Liquid Glass 2.0) with many dead/duplicate utilities; tailwind.config still carries an entire orphaned 2025 "deep space neon" palette (space/neon/tv colors, gradient-space/nebula/aurora) that conflicts with the token palette; lib/models.ts is a SECOND parallel color source with per-engine hex values (#05B878, #FF5947, #FEB113, #5DCBD8…) that do NOT match the canonical up/down/primary tokens and drive ~225 inline-hex literals (charts, badges); and 230 text-white usages plus dozens of bg-white/[x] are only made light-safe by ~60 lines of !important override hacks. Light theme is functionally complete but achieved via brute-force overrides rather than clean tokens. The aesthetic is competent but generic-AI/LuxAlgo-clone (glass, aurora blobs, gradient text, neon glows) rather than the distinctive look the redesign wants.

inventory.name: tokens.ts (canonical TS token source)
inventory.notes: Single source of truth: color (dark+light pairs), spacing (px–64px), radius (sm 4px→2xl 16px, full), type (sizes 11–34px, weights, line-heights), motion (durations 150/200/300ms, eases), z-index scale. Primary=#2BD9BC/#08A085. Exports MONO + AI(#8B5CF6) string consts. Comments claim runtime CSS vars are 'kept in sync' with this — true for colors, but tokens.ts is reference-only, not imported by runtime styling.

inventory.name: tailwind.config.ts
inventory.notes: darkMode:'class' (effectively dead — 0 dark: variants). colors map token names (main/wrap/line/d-text-*) to var(--color-*). BUT also carries a whole orphaned 2025 palette: space{void/deep/nebula}, neon{cyan/green/purple/pink/gold}, tv{blue/green/red/orange} with hardcoded hex; gradient-space/nebula/aurora/primary/glass backgroundImages; ~30 animations + keyframes (mesh/blob/aurora/starfield/spotlight…); glow/glass boxShadows. addVariablesForColors plugin re-exposes every color as a --var (used by Aceternity-style components). Two parallel font scales (token 11–34px vs tailwind 0.75rem–6rem + display-* ).

inventory.name: globals.css
inventory.notes: 2,528 lines. Sections: :root dark tokens + shadcn HSL compat (L10–73); html.light overrides (L85–140) + editorial-shell; base reset; 2-variant card system + 5 button variants (btn-primary/secondary/ghost/gradient/tv-gradient with shimmer ::after); gradient-text-* (6 variants); skeleton/loader/status-dot/ticker; glass-card-premium/nav/sidebar/topbar; glow/gradient-border-animated/card-shine; bento-grid; ~60 lines of html.light !important overrides for text-white/bg-white/border-white/ring-white opacity utilities; noise-overlay SVG; LuxAlgo lux-glass-card; Liquid Glass 2.0 .lg-* (ambient/surface/bar/ring/glow-ai using color-mix); print styles; mobile safe-area. 33 keyframes (pulse-ring defined twice). Heavy era-layering and dead utilities.

inventory.name: Liquid Glass 2.0 (.lg-* utilities)
inventory.notes: lg-ambient (fixed aurora blobs behind shell), lg-surface (translucent card, 42 uses), lg-bar (2), lg-ring (3), glow-ai (1), .lift micro-interaction. Token-driven via color-mix(var(--color-*)). Has @supports fallback to flat panels for old engines. The newest + cleanest layer; AppShell mounts lg-ambient.

inventory.name: ThemeToggle.tsx
inventory.notes: Two exports: ThemeToggle (Light/Dark/System radiogroup, used in Settings→Appearance) + ThemeToggleCompact (single-button toggle). Uses next-themes useTheme; mount-guarded to avoid hydration mismatch. Uses token classes (border-d-border, bg-wrap, text-primary).

inventory.name: appFont.ts (app-scoped fonts)
inventory.notes: Inter (--font-app-sans) + JetBrains Mono (--font-app-mono) loaded via next/font/google, applied ONLY on AppShell root. Authenticated app = Inter/JetBrains; marketing/auth = DM Sans/DM Mono (root layout). Dual typography system by design.

inventory.name: Root fonts (layout.tsx)
inventory.notes: DM_Sans(--font-sans) + DM_Mono(--font-mono, weights 300/400/500). <html className='light' suppressHydrationWarning>. body has noise-overlay + bg-main. themeColor #0A0D14. viewportFit cover for iOS notch.

inventory.name: providers.tsx (next-themes + spotlight)
inventory.notes: ThemeProvider attribute='class' defaultTheme='light' enableSystem storageKey='quantx.theme' disableTransitionOnChange. useCardSpotlight (rAF pointermove → --mouse-x/y for .glass-card::before). ThemedToaster (sonner) with INLINE hardcoded hex styles (#1C1E29, #E5E1D5, #1A1D24) — not tokenized.

inventory.name: lib/models.ts (parallel color source)
inventory.notes: SECOND design source: PUBLIC_MODELS registry with per-engine hex (#4FECCD,#5DCBD8,#FF9900,#FFD166,#05B878,#FF5947,#00E5CC,#8D5CFF) that do NOT match tokens.ts up/down/primary. Drives ~225 inline-hex literals. ALSO contains deprecated public names PatternScope + InsightAI (memory says descriptive names were blocked/deprecated). Mismatch + stale-naming hazard.

inventory.name: engines.ts (public engine names)
inventory.notes: 4 engines: Alpha/Mood/Regime/AutoPilot. Brand-firewall honored (no TFT/Qlib/FinBERT/HMM). NOTE: context brief expects 5 public engines incl. Counterpoint — Counterpoint exists only in lib/models.ts (debate_engine), not in ENGINES[]. Minor inventory drift between the two name sources.

inventory.name: check_frontend_hex_literals.sh (guard)
inventory.notes: Pre-commit guard blocking new (bg|text|border|ring|fill|stroke)-[#hex] Tailwind classes in frontend/app + components. Allowlist tiny (page.tsx, autopilot/page.tsx for #5DCBD8). Only catches Tailwind arbitrary-value classes — does NOT catch inline style={{}} hex or lib/models.ts hex (the actual bulk).

strengths: Theming is genuinely token-driven through ~25 CSS variables: a redesign can re-skin the entire app (both themes) by editing the :root and html.light variable blocks in globals.css — no need to touch 1700+ call-sites. This is the single most important asset for the redesign.

Strong semantic-token adoption across the app: text-primary (1145), text-d-text-primary (767), border tokens (692), bg-wrap (313), bg-main (163). The L0–L4 depth system (main/wrap/wrap-hover/hover/line/wrap-line) is consistent and well-documented.

Both themes are first-class and functionally complete — dark (deep navy-black #0B0E15) and light (cool off-white #F6F8FB) — with WCAG-AA-conscious text tiers (muted bumped to ~4.6:1) and AA-adjusted semantic colors per theme.
A pre-commit guard (check_frontend_hex_literals.sh) already enforces 'no new arbitrary-hex Tailwind classes', which is why arbitrary bg-[#hex] usage is contained to 25 occurrences.
The newest .lg-* Liquid Glass layer is clean, token-driven (color-mix on var(--color-*)) and has a proper @supports fallback — a good model for how the rest of the system should be built.

next-themes is correctly configured (storageKey namespaced, defaultTheme light, enableSystem, disableTransitionOnChange, suppressHydrationWarning) with mount-guards in toggles/toaster to avoid hydration mismatch.
Sensible scales exist for type/spacing/radius/motion/z-index in tokens.ts, and dual font system (Inter+JetBrains in app, DM Sans+DM Mono in marketing) is intentional and wired via CSS variables.

Good accessibility hygiene baked into CSS: prefers-reduced-motion blanket override, focus-visible outlines, mobile 16px input (iOS zoom fix), safe-area utilities, 44px touch targets, print stylesheet.

issues.title: Intent 'AI Trading OS' palette (#00E6A7 emerald-teal) is NOT implemented — actual signature is #2BD9BC/#08A085
issues.detail: The redesign brief specifies signature emerald-teal #00E6A7, bg #080B12, bull #00FF9D, bear #FF5B7F. None of these are the live values: page bg is #0B0E15 (dark)/#F6F8FB (light), primary is #2BD9BC/#08A085, up is #16C995/#07A368, down is #F23645/#DE2A40. The intent hexes appear only twice in the whole codebase, as color-mix() fallbacks in MarketProfileCard.tsx. The redesign must decide whether to adopt #00E6A7 (then change ~3 CSS vars) or formally retire the intent palette in the brief.

issues.title: lib/models.ts is a parallel, conflicting color source driving ~225 inline-hex literals
issues.detail: PUBLIC_MODELS carries per-engine hex (#05B878 86×, #FF5947 85×, #FEB113 54×, #4FECCD 29× etc.) used directly in inline style={{}} props (charts, badges, recharts fills). These bull/bear values (#05B878 up, #FF5947 down) do NOT match the canonical up/down tokens (#16C995/#F23645), so P&L green/red is INCONSISTENT depending on whether a component reads the token or models.ts. The hex-guard script does not catch inline-style hex, so this leaks freely. A redesign re-theming via tokens will leave all these surfaces on the OLD palette.

issues.title: globals.css is 2,528 lines of overlapping design eras with significant dead/duplicate code
issues.detail: At least 4 stacked eras coexist: deep-space-neon (glow-orb, bg-nebula, ambient-glow, retro-grid, starfield), LuxAlgo glass (lux-glass-card, lux-gradient-text), flat trading-surfaces (trading-surface, flattened glass-card), and Liquid Glass 2.0 (.lg-*). Many utilities are legacy back-compat aliases explicitly kept to avoid churn (glass-card-neu, btn-tv-gradient, gradient-text-professional). pulse-ring keyframe is defined twice; glass-card-premium noted as duplicate. ~13 gradient-text/lux/chrome variants. This is high maintenance surface for a redesign and risks the new system layering as era #5.

issues.title: 230 text-white + dozens of bg-white/[x] only kept light-safe by ~60 !important override hacks
issues.detail: Because so much markup hardcodes text-white / bg-white/[0.04] / text-white/60 / border-white/[0.06] / ring-white/10 (dark-mode assumptions), globals.css carries a large block of `html.light .text-white { color: ... !important }` style overrides (L1142–1216) mapping each opacity step to translucent ink. This is brittle (every new opacity step needs a new override line), fights specificity, and means light-mode correctness is patched rather than tokenized. A redesign should replace text-white with a semantic text token.

issues.title: tailwind.config carries an entire orphaned 2025 palette conflicting with the token system
issues.detail: space{void #04060e/deep/nebula}, neon{cyan/green/purple/pink/gold}, tv{blue/green/red/orange} colors plus gradient-space/nebula/aurora/primary/success/danger/gold backgroundImages are hardcoded hex and belong to the pre-token 'Deep Space' aesthetic. They coexist with the var(--color-*) token colors, giving two competing color vocabularies. Unclear how many are still referenced; they bloat the config and confuse the source of truth.

issues.title: darkMode:'class' is dead config — theming uses an inverted :root=dark / html.light strategy
issues.detail: There are ZERO Tailwind dark: variants in the codebase and NO html.dark selector in globals.css. Dark is the :root default; light is achieved by adding .light. So Tailwind's darkMode:'class' machinery is unused. This is fine (and actually simplifies re-theming) but is undocumented config debt — a future dev adding a dark: utility would find it silently never activates (next-themes adds .light/.dark but only .light is styled).

issues.title: Two parallel, divergent scale systems (tokens.ts vs tailwind.config)
issues.detail: tokens.ts type sizes are 11–34px (dense fintech) while tailwind.config fontSize is 0.75rem–6rem + display-sm/md/lg/xl (marketing). Spacing mostly agrees but tailwind adds 7/20/24/32 not in tokens. radius differs (tokens sm=4px; tailwind adds 4xl/5xl). Components pick whichever, so 'the type scale' is ambiguous. The redesign should collapse to one authoritative scale.

issues.title: Hardcoded hex inside inline styles and provider components bypasses all tokens
issues.detail: ThemedToaster in providers.tsx hardcodes #1C1E29/#E5E1D5/#1A1D24/rgba surfaces; globals.css itself hardcodes dozens of raw hex (#4FECCD, #8D5CFF, #1C1E29, #2D303D, #111520, #10121d) in glass/glow/lux utilities instead of var(--color-*). So even 'system' CSS is not fully tokenized — re-theming the variables won't recolor these surfaces.

issues.title: Public engine name drift + stale deprecated names in models.ts
issues.detail: engines.ts lists 4 engines (Alpha/Mood/Regime/AutoPilot); brief + models.ts imply 5 (incl. Counterpoint). models.ts still defines PatternScope and InsightAI public names which memory records as deprecated/blocked descriptive names. Two name registries (engines.ts vs models.ts) risk inconsistent labels in UI.

issues.title: Generic-AI / LuxAlgo-clone aesthetic vs the distinctive look the redesign wants
issues.detail: The implemented visual language (aurora blobs, gradient teal→purple text, neon glow shadows, glassmorphism, conic gradient borders, shimmer beams, noise overlay) is competent but is the default 2024–2025 'AI fintech' look — exactly the generic-AI aesthetic to avoid. The brief's DNA target (LuxAlgo/Intellectia/Cursor/Perplexity/Vercel) is only partially reflected (heavy LuxAlgo, little Cursor/Vercel restraint).

redesign_implications: BIGGEST LEVER: the system is genuinely re-themeable via ~25 CSS variables in globals.css (:root for dark, html.light for light). Adopting #00E6A7/#080B12/#00FF9D/#FF5B7F (or any new palette) is a ~30-line change to the variable blocks + tokens.ts — the 1700+ token-class call-sites flip automatically. Do this FIRST and verify visually.

BUT re-theming variables alone will NOT recolor: (a) ~225 inline-hex literals fed by lib/models.ts, (b) the off-token bull/bear chart colors (#05B878/#FF5947), (c) hardcoded hex inside globals.css glass/glow utilities, (d) the orphaned space/neon/tv tailwind palette. These must be migrated to tokens or they'll visibly diverge from the new look.

Reconcile lib/models.ts hex into the token system (or delete its hex field) so engine/chart colors come from one source. Also align up/down P&L colors — pick ONE green and ONE red and route everything through --color-up/--color-down.
Replace the 230 text-white usages with a semantic --color-light text token; this lets you delete the ~60-line html.light !important override block and makes both themes clean rather than patched.

Treat globals.css as a rewrite candidate, not an extension target. It has 4+ stacked eras of dead/legacy utilities. Adding the new design as era #5 will compound the bloat. Prefer: extract the variable blocks + the .lg-* layer (keep), audit-and-delete the deep-space-neon + redundant gradient-text/lux/glass utilities.

Decide the theming mechanism explicitly: either keep the inverted :root=dark/html.light strategy (document it, remove dead darkMode:'class') OR move to standard Tailwind dark: variants. Don't leave both half-present.
Consolidate the two scale systems into one authoritative type/spacing/radius scale (tokens.ts is the better dense-fintech base; tailwind's display-* sizes are marketing-only).

Lean into restraint (Cursor/Vercel DNA) to escape the generic-AI look: the current aurora/neon-glow/gradient-text vocabulary is the cliché to avoid. The .lg-* Liquid Glass layer + the flat trading-surface are the more defensible directions to build on.
Brand firewall is intact in engines.ts — preserve it. Resolve the Alpha/Mood/Regime/AutoPilot(+Counterpoint) name set in ONE registry before the redesign relabels surfaces.
reusable_assets: The CSS-variable token contract in globals.css (:root + html.light): --color-main/wrap/wrap-hover/hover/line/wrap-line (L0–L4 depth), --color-light/desc/muted (text tiers), --color-primary/up/down/warning/highlight/orange/ai/cyan, --chart-* tokens, and shadcn HSL compat vars. This IS the re-theme surface — keep the contract, swap the values.
tokens.ts as the canonical TS reference for spacing (px–64px), radius, motion (durations/eases), z-index — reuse directly; just reconcile the type scale.
The .lg-* Liquid Glass 2.0 utilities (lg-ambient/lg-surface/lg-bar/lg-ring/glow-ai/.lift) — clean, token-driven via color-mix, with @supports fallback. The pattern to standardize on.
ThemeToggle / ThemeToggleCompact components and the next-themes setup (storageKey quantx.theme, defaultTheme light, enableSystem, mount-guards) — fully reusable as-is.
Dual font wiring: appFont.ts (Inter + JetBrains Mono via --font-app-sans/mono on AppShell) and root DM Sans/DM Mono — keep the variable-based loading; only swap families if the redesign changes type.

Accessibility/responsive CSS primitives: prefers-reduced-motion override, focus-visible outline, safe-area utilities (safe-top/bottom/x), min-h-screen-dvh, touch-target, mobile 16px input fix, print stylesheet — reuse verbatim.
check_frontend_hex_literals.sh pre-commit guard — keep and extend it to also catch inline style hex and lib/models.ts hex.
The flat trading-surface / card-dark / glass-card (flattened) primitives for data-dense panels — solid base for the data UI; just re-point their hardcoded hex to tokens.
Stagger/reveal/skeleton-shimmer animation utilities and the bento-grid layout system — generic enough to carry forward.

res.summary: The frontend component library is split into two clearly distinct quality tiers. The `components/foundation/*` layer (27 files) is genuinely excellent: a disciplined, token-driven, documented (README + JSDoc) primitive set — Button/Card/Badge/Input/Select/NumericInput/Sheet/Dialog/ConfirmDialog/Tabs/Tooltip/Popover/Toast/DataTable/StatCard/Sparkline/ChangeBadge/EmptyState/PageHeader/Skeleton/UsageMeter plus the v4 glass primitives (Reveal/Verdict/Spark/DisclaimerFooter). Radix-backed where it matters (Dialog/Sheet/Select/Tabs/Tooltip/Popover), cva used in Sheet, all consume CSS-variable Tailwind tokens, strong a11y (focus rings, aria, keyboard nav, loading/empty/error baked in). This is the right base to keep for the redesign. The feature dirs (~25 of them, ~104 files) are a mixed bag: newer surfaces (scanner, signals, copilot GenUI) correctly compose foundation primitives and use the `lg-*` glass + AI-purple language; but large swathes (managed, stock, landing, shell internals) bypass foundation with 125 raw `<button>` instances and inconsistent styling. The token system itself is the biggest liability: `tailwind.config.ts` carries an entire dead legacy "Deep Space / neon fintech" theme (space/neon/tv color families, aurora/starfield/blob animations, glass shadows) layered on top of the current AI Trading OS tokens, AND it maps `success`/`danger` to undefined CSS variables — so `text-success`/`bg-danger`/`stroke-success` used across the entire managed/ beginner dir and several stock cards render with NO color (a live visual bug). Charting is fragmented across three approaches: TradingView lightweight-charts (with its own hardcoded palette that doesn't even match tokens.ts), recharts (3 files), and hand-rolled SVG sparklines. framer-motion is barely used (4 files); most motion is CSS. The brand-firewall (no model names) and SEBI disclaimer discipline are intact.

inventory.name: foundation/Button
inventory.notes: Custom (not shadcn), forwardRef, variants primary/secondary/ghost/danger/ai + sm/md/lg. Token classes only. The canonical button — but only ~30 files import foundation; 125 raw <button> exist elsewhere.

inventory.name: foundation/Card + CardHeader/Body/Footer
inventory.notes: Custom composition. variant static|clickable|glass; glass maps to .lg-surface lift. Clean, reusable.

inventory.name: foundation/Badge + Verdict
inventory.notes: 9 tones incl buy/hold/sell + ai (color-mix tints, theme-aware). Verdict.tsx is a thin BUY/HOLD/SELL wrapper — good single-source-of-truth pattern.

inventory.name: foundation/Input + NumericInput
inventory.notes: Custom. NumericInput is trading-grade: onWheel-blur guard, formatter (currency-inr/percent/multiplier), steppers, clamp, intermediate-typing raw state. Reusable as-is.

inventory.name: foundation/Select
inventory.notes: Radix-backed, label/error/helper, animate-in. Solid. No Combobox/typeahead variant yet (noted as future).

inventory.name: foundation/Dialog + ConfirmDialog + Sheet
inventory.notes: All Radix-dialog-backed. Sheet uses cva for side variants. ConfirmDialog focuses Cancel (safe-Enter) for destructive actions. sr-only Description a11y handled.

inventory.name: foundation/DataTable
inventory.notes: Generic<Row>, sortable, sticky header/col, loading-skeletons/empty/error slots, keyboard row activation, aria-sort. Best primitive in the lib. ~10 usages. No virtualization (notes >5000 rows need react-window).

inventory.name: foundation/StatCard
inventory.notes: KPI cell, composes ChangeBadge+Sparkline+Skeleton+Tooltip. loading/error baked in. glass prop. ~8 usages.

inventory.name: foundation/Sparkline + Spark
inventory.notes: TWO sparkline primitives: Sparkline (token stroke/fill classes, auto-tone) and Spark (gradient-area, takes raw color string). Redundant — consolidate in redesign.

inventory.name: foundation/Tooltip + Popover
inventory.notes: Radix-backed, clear separation (Tooltip=hint, Popover=interactive). Quiet animate-in motion. Clean.

inventory.name: foundation/Toast
inventory.notes: Thin Sonner re-export (single import chokepoint). Good.

inventory.name: foundation/EmptyState + PageHeader + ChangeBadge + UsageMeter + DisclaimerFooter
inventory.notes: Layout/data-display primitives. ChangeBadge does Indian lakh/crore numbering + auto-tone+arrow. UsageMeter for tier caps. DisclaimerFooter = SEBI compliance invariant. All token-driven.

inventory.name: foundation/README.md
inventory.notes: Strong design-system doc: catalogue, state-coverage matrix, trading conventions, 'never inline a button' rules, hex-literal ban. Mandate is only ~30% adopted across the app.

inventory.name: shell/AppShell + Sidebar + NavList + Topbar + MobileDrawer
inventory.notes: The redesign center. Header-less desktop (Topbar mobile-only), collapsible glass Sidebar (brand/new-chat/nav/recent-chats/footer), lg-ambient aurora, skip-to-content, ⌘K palette. NavList is dual-mode chokepoint (managed vs pro). MUST-PRESERVE IA. Brand logo gradient hardcoded #2BD9BC/#00E5FF/#04140F inline (Sidebar+Topbar).

inventory.name: shell/CommandPalette + AutopilotStickyStop
inventory.notes: Not opened in this pass; CommandPalette wired to ⌘K in AppShell. AutopilotStickyStop = persistent kill-switch surface.

inventory.name: copilot/EmbeddedAgent
inventory.notes: Streaming-choreography engine (typing→tool-trace→token-by-token→staggered artifacts→actions). STATIC vs LIVE (run()) modes, honest error+Retry. Reusable across feature pages. Hardcodes #8B5CF6 once (line 165) + uses AI token via inline style throughout.

inventory.name: copilot/artifacts
inventory.notes: GenUI primitives: ChipRow/ArtifactCard/Bars/StatPills/Gauge/ActionRow. framer-motion staggered reveals. Theme-aware via CSS vars + AI token. The bento/GenUI vocabulary for the redesign.

inventory.name: copilot/CopilotProvider + types
inventory.notes: Tok/ChipItem types shared by EmbeddedAgent+artifacts. CopilotProvider context.

inventory.name: charts/LightweightChart
inventory.notes: TradingView lightweight-charts candle+volume+EMA21/50/200+pattern overlay. ENTIRELY inline styles + its own hardcoded PALETTE that does NOT match tokens.ts (up #05B878 vs token #16C995, etc). Not theme-token-driven. Heavy but functional.

inventory.name: charts/VolumeProfilePanel
inventory.notes: Not opened; second charting component.

inventory.name: recharts users (EquityCurveWithBenchmark, BacktestViewer, PayoffDiagram)
inventory.notes: 3 files use recharts for equity/backtest/payoff. Third charting approach alongside lightweight-charts and SVG sparklines — fragmented.

inventory.name: signals/SignalCard + DebateTranscript + CategorySignalsPage + ModelAccuracyCard + ExplanationMarkdown
inventory.notes: SignalCard composes foundation Badge/ChangeBadge/Sparkline + lg-surface; uses CSS-var inline tone (var(--color-up) etc, not hex) — good. Exemplar feature card.

inventory.name: managed/* (HealthScoreCard, AutopilotCard, MoneyCard, RiskRegimeCard, format)
inventory.notes: Beginner 'Managed' mode cards. Compose foundation Card. BUT use text-success/text-danger/stroke-success/bg-success/bg-danger + format.ts — these map to UNDEFINED --success/--danger CSS vars → render with no color. Live visual bug on the beginner home.

inventory.name: stock/* (15 cards: FusionVerdict, AIDossier, ChartVision, Fundamentals, NewsIntelligence, etc.)
inventory.notes: Rich per-symbol analysis cards composing foundation Card. FusionVerdictCard + NewsIntelligenceCard use dead text-success/bg-success/text-danger classes (same undefined-var bug). Otherwise token-clean.

inventory.name: scanner/* (11 tabs: PatternsV2, PowerScreeners, Intraday, Fno, News, MultiTimeframe, SavedScans, SectorHeatmap, FactorScreener, SetupFinder, LiveAlerts)
inventory.notes: Largest feature dir, correctly imports Badge/Button/DataTable/EmptyState/Skeleton from foundation. Best-behaved feature dir.

inventory.name: fno/* (6: DerivativesAnalysis, OiHeatmap, OptionsCopilot, OptionsFlow, PayoffCalculator, FnoStockScanners)
inventory.notes: F&O surfaces; not opened in detail. PayoffDiagram (strategy/) uses recharts.

inventory.name: markets/* (BreadthCard, MarketExplainer, OrderFlow, SectorRotation, StockMoodLookup)
inventory.notes: Market-wide cards; some embed agents (MarketExplainer).

inventory.name: landing/* (LightNavbar, Footer, BrandCarousel + 4 more)
inventory.notes: SEPARATE older design system. Hardcodes hex (#00F0FF/#9250FF/#0D8ED6/#FEB113/#05B878), gradient-to-[#5DCBD8]. References STALE routes (/momentum, /swingmax-signal, /assistant, /analytics, /marketplace, /scanner-lab) and old engine framing. Source of most of the 18 hex-literal violations.

inventory.name: auth/AuthLayout + ClientAuthGate
inventory.notes: AuthLayout hardcodes multiple hex gradients/glows (#0A0D14,#111520,#8D5CFF,rgba(79,236,205)). Off the token system. ClientAuthGate = dev bypass per memory.

inventory.name: ui/PillTabs + StockAvatar
inventory.notes: PillTabs uses bg-surface-2 (ok) but bg-hover (ok) — duplicates foundation Tabs. StockAvatar references bg-dot-blue/dot-purple/dot-indigo which are UNDEFINED tokens → broken avatar colors. Sparse 'ui' dir (2 files) vs 'foundation'.

inventory.name: shared/SystemHaltBanner
inventory.notes: Polls system status, bg-down halt banner. Clean, token-driven. shared/ dir is nearly empty (1 component).

inventory.name: theme/ThemeToggle (+Compact)
inventory.notes: 3-way Light/Dark/System via next-themes, in Settings per IA. Uses border-d-border alias + one hardcoded rgba(11,163,127,0.10) shadow.

inventory.name: dashboard/QuickTrade + IndexCards + Day1WelcomeBanner
inventory.notes: QuickTrade is one of only 4 framer-motion users. Dashboard primitives.

inventory.name: paper/journal/portfolio/strategies/strategy cards
inventory.notes: Paper-trade league, equity curves (recharts), trade-review, backtest viewer, DSL preview, payoff diagram. StrategyCompareCard uses dead success/danger classes.

strengths: foundation/* is a genuinely strong, documented design-primitive layer (27 files, README + per-file JSDoc with 2+ examples). Radix-backed overlays/forms, cva in Sheet, full loading/empty/error/disabled/focus state coverage, strong a11y (aria-sort, role/keyboard activation, skip-to-content, sr-only descriptions, ≥44px tap targets).
DataTable is excellent: generic-typed, client+controlled sort, sticky header/first-column, skeleton/empty/error slots, keyboard nav — directly reusable and the right contract for every list surface.

Trading-domain primitives are thoughtful: ChangeBadge (Indian lakh/crore + auto tone/arrow), NumericInput (onWheel-blur guard, formatters, clamping), Verdict (BUY/HOLD/SELL single source of truth), DisclaimerFooter (SEBI compliance).

The GenUI/streaming layer (EmbeddedAgent + artifacts) is sophisticated and reusable: real STATIC-vs-LIVE choreography, honest error+Retry, ChipRow/Bars/StatPills/Gauge/ArtifactCard bento vocabulary — a strong foundation for the AI-forward redesign.

Shell IA already matches the stated design intent: header-less desktop, collapsible glass sidebar, dual-mode (managed/pro) NavList chokepoint, ⌘K palette, lg-ambient aurora, recent-chats grouping. The hard structural work is done.
Newer feature dirs (scanner 11 files, signals) consistently compose foundation primitives and the lg-* glass + AI-purple language — proving the system works when followed.
Brand-firewall and tokens.ts intent are coherent: AI-purple reserved via the `ai` token, BUY/SELL semantics, theme-aware color-mix tints, both dark+light first-class in CSS vars.

issues.title: Dead `success`/`danger` tokens render with NO color across Managed beginner mode + several stock/strategy cards
issues.detail: tailwind.config.ts maps `success: rgb(var(--success)/<alpha>)` and `danger: rgb(var(--danger)/<alpha>)`, but --success and --danger are NEVER defined in globals.css (only --warning, --destructive exist). So `text-success`/`bg-success`/`stroke-success`/`text-danger`/`bg-danger` resolve to `rgb(var(--success))` = invalid = browser-default/inherited color. This is the live AutoPilot status dot, the portfolio HealthScore ring/labels, the Fusion verdict lean colors — exactly the beginner-facing surfaces. The redesign must replace all of these with up/down/warning tokens or define the vars.

issues.title: tailwind.config.ts carries a large DEAD legacy theme bloating the token surface
issues.detail: The config still defines an entire 'Deep Space / 2026 neon fintech' system — space/neon/tv color families, glass.* colors, gradient-space/nebula/aurora/glow-* shadows, and ~20 keyframes (starfield, blob, meshFlow, aurora, ringSpin, dockBounce...) — none of which belong to the current AI Trading OS palette. This coexists with the real tokens and with the legacy shadcn-style hsl vars (--primary/--secondary/--accent/--card/--ring) that duplicate the --color-* system. Two-and-a-half overlapping token systems make it impossible to reason about which class is canonical. The redesign should prune to one system.

issues.title: Undefined `dot-*` tokens break StockAvatar colors
issues.detail: StockAvatar.tsx cycles COLORS including bg-dot-blue/text-dot-blue, bg-dot-purple, bg-dot-indigo — none of which are defined in tailwind.config.ts or globals.css. The avatar background/text colors silently fail for ~half its color slots.

issues.title: Foundation 'never inline a button' mandate is only ~30% adopted
issues.detail: The README mandates importing only from @/components/foundation, yet only ~30 of ~131 component files do, and there are 125 raw <button> instances. managed/, stock/, landing/, and shell internals roll their own buttons/inputs with bespoke className strings, producing subtle inconsistency (padding, radius, hover, focus-ring). Newer dirs (scanner/signals) comply; older ones don't.

issues.title: Charting is fragmented across 3 approaches with a non-token palette
issues.detail: LightweightChart (TradingView candles) uses 100% inline styles and its own hardcoded PALETTE that does NOT match tokens.ts (up=#05B878 vs token #16C995, ema/crosshair hexes). recharts powers 3 other components (equity/backtest/payoff). Plus two hand-rolled SVG sparklines (Sparkline + Spark) that are themselves redundant. No single charting contract or shared palette; theme-switching the lightweight chart is manual via a `theme` prop, not the global token system.

issues.title: Landing components are a separate, dated design system with hardcoded hex and stale routes
issues.detail: landing/* (LightNavbar, Footer, BrandCarousel) live off the token system: hardcoded #00F0FF/#9250FF/#0D8ED6/#FEB113/#5DCBD8, and the LightNavbar mega-menu links to STALE/removed routes (/momentum, /swingmax-signal, /assistant, /analytics, /marketplace, /scanner-lab) and old engine framing. These account for most of the 18 hex-literal violations the pre-commit hook is supposed to block (they predate it). The marketing surface needs its own redesign pass distinct from the app shell.

issues.title: Brand logo gradient + a few accents hardcoded inline instead of tokenized
issues.detail: Sidebar.tsx and Topbar.tsx hardcode the Q-logo gradient `linear-gradient(135deg,#2BD9BC,#00E5FF)` + `#04140F` inline; EmbeddedAgent hardcodes #8B5CF6 once (line 165); ThemeToggle has a rgba(11,163,127,0.10) shadow. These bypass tokens.ts/globals.css and won't theme. Minor but should be tokenized in the redesign (e.g. a --brand-gradient var).

issues.title: Duplicated/overlapping primitives: PillTabs vs foundation Tabs, Spark vs Sparkline, ui/ vs foundation/
issues.detail: PillTabs (ui/) duplicates foundation Tabs with a different look; Spark duplicates Sparkline; the near-empty `ui/` (2 files) and `shared/` (1 file) dirs fragment the primitive story alongside the canonical `foundation/`. The redesign should collapse these into foundation and delete the strays.

redesign_implications: KEEP and extend foundation/* as the design-primitive layer — it is the single biggest asset and already token-driven, a11y-complete, and documented. A token-swap (CSS vars + tokens.ts) re-themes everything that goes through it. The redesign's job is to (a) drive ADOPTION (migrate the 125 raw <button> sites + managed/stock/landing onto foundation) and (b) refresh the visual values, not rebuild the primitives.

FIX the token system FIRST, before any visual work: prune the dead legacy 'Deep Space/neon' theme + the duplicate shadcn hsl vars from tailwind.config.ts, define (or remove) --success/--danger or globally rename usages to up/down/warning, and define the dot-* tokens or drop StockAvatar's broken slots. Otherwise the redesign inherits silent color-failure bugs on the beginner home.

The shell IA (header-less desktop, collapsible glass Sidebar, dual-mode NavList, ⌘K palette, lg-ambient aurora, recent-chats) already implements the stated intent — PRESERVE the structure and re-skin, do not re-architect. NavList is the single mode chokepoint (managed vs pro) and must stay that way.

The GenUI/streaming layer (EmbeddedAgent + artifacts) is the AI-forward differentiator and matches the Intellectia/Cursor DNA target — build the redesign's agent surfaces on these existing primitives (ChipRow/Bars/StatPills/Gauge/ArtifactCard + the STATIC/LIVE run() choreography) rather than inventing new ones.

Consolidate charting into one contract: pick a shared token-driven palette for LightweightChart (currently a divergent hardcoded PALETTE), decide recharts-vs-lightweight per use-case, and merge Spark into Sparkline. The redesign is the moment to define a single chart theming story tied to the global tokens + theme toggle.

Treat the marketing/landing surface as a SEPARATE redesign track (per memory: FinStocks aesthetic for marketing, Tradomate/cockpit for app). Landing currently has its own stale hex+routes and should not share the cockpit token migration blindly — but its stale route links and removed-engine framing must be reconciled with the current route map.

res.summary: The marketing + onboarding surfaces are competent and content-rich but carry serious correctness and freshness debt that a redesign must fix. The landing page (app/page.tsx) has a clean 7-section structure with live public-endpoint data (regime pill, track-record bar, signal-of-the-day, cumulative-return sparkline) and tasteful decorative blobs — genuinely good. BUT the entire marketing tree (landing, FeatureGrid, HowItWorks, PricingPreview, LightNavbar) hardcodes text-white and dark-assumed surfaces (bg-wrap = #FFFFFF in light theme) while the app's default theme is LIGHT (providers.tsx defaultTheme='light', no forcedTheme on these pages) — so in a fresh visitor's default light theme these pages render white-text-on-white and are effectively broken. This is the single biggest issue. Second, there are two competing brand-firewall violations: app/page.tsx hero says "12 AI models" and lists engines "Forecast · Alpha · Gate · Regime · Mood" while AuthLayout and signup say "6 ML models" / "6 AI models" — neither matches the locked public set (Alpha · Mood · Regime · AutoPilot · Counterpoint), and the FeatureComparisonMatrix literally prints internal model labels in feature names. Third, the signup page is badly stale: Elite ₹2,499 (locked is ₹1,999), "5 signals per day" on Free (locked is 1/day), a fabricated "Join 5,000+ traders" and "73%+ Win Rate" social-proof number, and a 3-step plan-selection flow that collects a plan choice it never actually uses (handleFinalSignup ignores selectedPlan). The palette is also off-spec: navbar CTA + carousel hardcode cyan #00F0FF and landing uses teal #4FECCD, while the design intent and tokens.ts both say signature teal-green #2BD9BC/#00E6A7. Navbar mega-menu and Footer link to ~7 retired routes (/momentum, /scanner-lab, /swingmax-signal, /assistant, /marketplace, /analytics) that only resolve via 301 redirects, plus several dead href="#" footer links. Onboarding (risk-quiz → mode-choice → broker-connect → complete) is the strongest, most consistent, token-clean part of this whole area. Auth pages are polished but use a different token namespace (l-* light tokens) than the rest of the app and the OAuth callback uses undefined Tailwind tokens.

Decorative blobs are landing-only per spec. Hero badge says '12 AI models'; copy lists engines 'Forecast · Alpha · Gate · Regime · Mood' (off-spec brand set).

inventory.notes: Sticky nav, 3-group product mega-menu (hover, 200ms close timeout), live 30-day regime mini-timeline with per-cell tooltips + high-turnover pip + dismiss. Strong feature. strengths: Landing page is genuinely data-driven: hero chart, regime pill, 30-day regime timeline, track-record stat strip and cumulative-return sparkline all read live public CDN-cached endpoints with honest '—'/loading empty states (TrackRecordBar.tsx, HeroChartPlaceholder, LightNavbar regime strip).

Onboarding flow (risk-quiz → result → mode-choice → broker-connect → complete) is the cleanest, most consistent, fully tokenized part of the whole area — proper loading/error/skip states, dual-mode managed/pro choice, cache invalidation, real OAuth wiring.

Auth forms are solid engineering: react-hook-form + zod validation, password strength meter, show/hide toggles, email-enumeration-safe forgot-password, correct Suspense boundaries around useSearchParams (signup + verify-email), Google OAuth.

Pricing page is conversion-mature: real Razorpay checkout, DB-derived yearly discount (no hardcoded lie), personalized quiz-recommendation banner with A/B variant tracking and per-session dismiss, 11-item FAQ, collapsible feature matrix.

Decorative restraint is correct: blobs/glass are confined to landing/pricing/auth per the spec, not bled into product surfaces. AuthLayout's left panel (candlesticks + retro grid + glows) is high-polish.

SEBI/risk disclaimers, kill-switch language, 'we never hold your funds', AES-256 broker-token framing are present and consistent across Footer/FAQ/Terms/Privacy — compliance posture is taken seriously.
tokens.ts + globals.css define a complete dual-theme (dark+light) 5-layer depth system with WCAG-AA-tuned text colors and .lg-* glass utilities — a strong foundation the redesign can reuse.

issues.title: Marketing pages break in the app's DEFAULT (light) theme — white text on white
issues.detail: providers.tsx sets defaultTheme='light' with no forcedTheme on marketing routes. Landing/FeatureGrid/HowItWorks/PricingPreview/PricingPreview/LightNavbar hardcode text-white and dark-assumed surfaces (bg-wrap resolves to #FFFFFF in light theme per globals.css html.light). A fresh visitor in default light theme sees white text on near-white cards across the hero, feature grid, pricing preview and nav — i.e. the primary acquisition funnel is effectively unreadable unless the user has previously chosen dark. 23 hardcoded text-white literals across landing components confirm a dark-only design that was never theme-aware.

issues.title: Brand-firewall leaks: internal model names + inconsistent model COUNT shown to users
issues.detail: Locked public set is Alpha·Mood·Regime·AutoPilot·Counterpoint. Yet: hero badge says '12 AI models' and copy lists 'Forecast · Alpha · Gate · Regime · Mood'; AuthLayout stat says '6 AI Models'; signup subtitle says '6 ML models' — three different counts, none canonical. FeatureComparisonMatrix prints internal labels in feature names ('Full 4-engine', '(F8) · Regime', '(F1) · Intraday'). The check_legacy_branding.sh hook only blocks old descriptive names (SwingLens etc.), not raw model counts/internal engine refs, so these slip through CI.

issues.title: Signup page is stale and structurally broken for conversion
issues.detail: Elite priced ₹2,499 (locked is ₹1,999 — directly contradicts /pricing and PricingPreview); Free lists '5 signals per day' (locked 1/day); fabricated 'Join 5,000+ traders' and AuthLayout '73%+ Win Rate' social proof with no data source. Worse: the 3-step wizard makes the user pick a plan (selectedPlan state, step 2 + step 3 summary) but handleFinalSignup never sends it — the choice is silently discarded, so the plan-selection step is pure friction with zero effect.

issues.title: Off-spec / inconsistent primary palette across marketing
issues.detail: Design intent + tokens.ts = signature teal-green #2BD9BC (dark) / #08A085 (light) / target #00E6A7. But LightNavbar CTA + mobile CTA + pill hardcode cyan bg-[#00F0FF]; BrandCarousel dot is #00F0FF; landing/FeatureGrid/Footer/AuthLayout hardcode teal #4FECCD and a grab-bag of accent hexes (#FEB113,#5DCBD8,#8D5CFF,#0D8ED6). Result: the most-seen CTA color is a cyan that exists nowhere in the token system.

issues.title: Conflicting Copilot message caps advertised on three surfaces
issues.detail: PricingPreview: Free 5 / Pro 50. pricing/page.tsx STATIC_PLANS: Free 5 / Pro 150 / Elite 400. FeatureComparisonMatrix: Free 5 / Pro 50 / Elite 200. Three different Pro/Elite numbers on the same product — a paying user will catch this. Memory notes the real backend caps are 5/150/400, so two of the three surfaces are wrong.

issues.title: Navbar + footer link to retired routes (301-only) and dead anchors
issues.detail: LightNavbar mega-menu + Footer Product column link to /momentum, /scanner-lab, /swingmax-signal, /assistant, /marketplace, /analytics — all retired and only resolved via middleware 301 redirects to different targets (e.g. Marketplace→/strategies, AI Assistant→/copilot). Users get a label-mismatch landing. Footer Resources (Docs/API/Blog/Changelog), Company (About/Contact/Careers), Legal (Risk Disclosure/Refund) and ALL 6 social links are href='#' — dead. Footer mega-menu also says '6 proprietary engines' — yet another model-count.

issues.title: Compliance contradiction: 'SEBI Registered' vs 'registration PENDING'
issues.detail: Footer prints '© Quant X. SEBI Registered Research Analyst.' while app/terms says 'pending SEBI Research Analyst registration. Reg # <PENDING — to be inserted post-approval>'. A visible placeholder token and a false 'Registered' claim on the same site is both a UX and a regulatory liability.

issues.title: Auth callback uses undefined Tailwind tokens (likely renders unstyled)
issues.detail: app/auth/callback uses bg-background, text-text-secondary, border-accent, text-accent — none exist in tailwind.config.ts (the app uses bg-main, d-text-secondary, primary, line). The OAuth completion spinner and text likely render transparent/black-default. Also no handling for the ?type=recovery deep-link the forgot-password flow sends here, so password reset has no landing form.

issues.title: Two divergent token namespaces between marketing and auth
issues.detail: Auth pages + AuthLayout use the l-* light namespace (l-text, l-border, l-bg-subtle, l-accent) while landing uses bg-main/bg-wrap/d-text-* and pricing uses d-bg/d-bg-card. Three styling dialects for one funnel makes a consistent redesign harder and risks visual drift between login and the landing it came from.

issues.title: Placeholder social proof instead of real assets
issues.detail: BrandCarousel renders broker NAMES as plain text with a dot, not real logos — reads as unfinished. AuthLayout '73%+/6/1,800+' and signup '5,000+ traders' are hardcoded with no backing endpoint (the app HAS live publicTrust endpoints these could use). Trust surfaces should be real or removed.

issues.title: Accessibility gaps in interactive marketing/onboarding controls
issues.detail: Navbar Products dropdown is hover-only with no aria-expanded/aria-haspopup and not keyboard-openable (button has no onClick/onKeyDown to toggle). Risk-quiz option buttons are radio-semantics rendered as plain buttons (no role=radio/aria-checked/fieldset). Forgot-password input label is not associated (no htmlFor/id). FeatureComparisonMatrix uses div-grid 'table' with no table semantics for screen readers.

issues.title: FeatureGrid says '12 AI features' but renders 10 cards
issues.detail: Header claims '12 AI features. Not 12 chatbot flavors.' but the FEATURES array has 10 entries (F5, F10 missing with no explanation). Mismatched counting undercuts the 'no fluff / everything is real' positioning.

issues.title: Inconsistent product naming and metadata drift
issues.detail: onboarding/complete + landing dropdown call /dashboard 'Command Center' / 'Open Dashboard' inconsistently. layout metadata themeColor #0A0D14 doesn't match token --color-main #0B0E15. Footer email @quantx.in vs Terms/Privacy @quantx.app vs legal@/privacy@quantx.app. No sitemap.ts/robots.ts despite a marketing site (only opengraph-image present).

redesign_implications: Decide theme policy for marketing up front: either force dark on all marketing/auth routes (wrap in a dark-locked shell) OR make every landing component theme-aware (replace text-white with text-d-text-primary, bg-wrap glass tuned for both themes). The current 'light default + dark-only components' combination is the #1 thing to fix and blocks any honest visual evaluation.

Collapse the three token dialects (landing bg-main/d-text-*, pricing d-bg/d-bg-card, auth l-*) into ONE semantic token set consumed via Tailwind classes from tokens.ts/globals.css. No raw hex in marketing components — there are dozens (#00F0FF, #4FECCD, #FEB113, #8D5CFF, #0D8ED6) to migrate.

Establish a single source of truth for product facts: tier prices, signal/day caps, Copilot message caps, model count, win-rate, user count. Right now prices, caps and model counts disagree across signup/PricingPreview/pricing/matrix/AuthLayout. Drive all of these from one config (ideally the backend FEATURE_MATRIX + live publicTrust stats) so a redesign can't reintroduce drift.

Extend the brand-firewall enforcement (check_legacy_branding.sh) to also fail on internal engine names (TFT/Qlib/FinBERT/HMM/'4-engine') AND on bare 'N AI models / N ML models' claims in user-facing files, so the redesign's copy can't leak them. Replace all engine refs in FeatureComparisonMatrix/HowItWorks/hero with the public set Alpha·Mood·Regime·AutoPilot·Counterpoint.

Rebuild signup conversion flow: either remove the no-op plan-selection step or actually wire selectedPlan into checkout; fix Elite to ₹1,999 and Free to 1 signal/day; replace fabricated social proof with real publicTrust data or drop it. The wizard currently adds friction with zero payoff.

Audit every marketing/footer link against the live route map. Either update labels to match where the 301 sends users (Marketplace→Strategies, AI Assistant→Copilot) or restore the routes; build out or hide the dead href='#' Resources/Company/Legal/social links before launch.

Resolve the SEBI compliance contradiction before the redesign ships any new footer/legal copy: pick 'Registered' vs 'pending' consistently and remove the visible '<PENDING>' placeholder. Treat legal copy as launch-blocking.
Fix the OAuth callback styling (undefined tokens) and add a ?type=recovery password-reset form so the forgot-password loop completes; this is part of the auth funnel and currently dead-ends visually.

Bake accessibility into the redesign system: keyboard-operable nav dropdown with aria-expanded, radiogroup semantics for the risk-quiz, table semantics (or real <table>) for the comparison matrix, associated labels on all inputs. The onboarding flow is a good baseline to standardize from.
reusable_assets: Live public-data wiring: api.publicTrust.{indices, regimeHistory, signalOfTheDay, trackRecord} consumed by HeroChartPlaceholder (app/page.tsx), TrackRecordBar.tsx and LightNavbar regime strip — keep these contracts; the redesign should reuse them for hero stats, social proof and the regime pill.
TrackRecordBar.tsx CurveSparkline + 5-stat Cell pattern (honest '—'/loading states, aria-labeled SVG) — directly reusable for any redesigned trust strip.

Onboarding engine: api.onboarding.{quiz, submit, status, skip} + lib/onboardingStatusCache (5-min TTL + invalidate) + lib/tierUpsell (quizRecCopy/quizRecReason/quizRecDelta/tierRank) + lib/abVariant (getVariant/reportExposure) + lib/reportUpgradeIntent — backend-driven quiz, recommendation banner and A/B funnel are reusable as-is.

Dual-mode ModeChoiceScreen pattern + useUiMode/setMode (contexts/UiModeContext, readCachedUiMode) — the managed-vs-pro split and the landing redirect gate (managed→/home, pro→/copilot) must be preserved.

Broker OAuth wiring: api.broker.initiateOAuth + sessionStorage state stash + /broker/callback handoff (onboarding/broker-connect) and components/broker/BrokerConnectTile.tsx (status pill state machine) + BrokerLock.tsx (.lg-surface gated empty-state) — keep contracts; only swap the placeholder single-letter logos for real broker marks.

Auth plumbing: useAuth (signIn/signUp/signInWithGoogle), AuthContext, lib/supabase, GoogleLogo, foundation barrel (Button/Card/CardBody/CardHeader/toast), react-hook-form+zod schemas, PasswordStrength meter, and the correct useSearchParams Suspense boundaries (signup/verify-email).

Design foundation: lib/tokens.ts (dual-theme 5-layer palette + spacing/radius) + globals.css .lg-* glass utilities, gradient-text-teal, illustration-glow-teal/purple, input-animated-wrapper, noise-overlay, num-display — the redesign should build on these rather than re-deriving a palette.
Razorpay checkout flow in pricing/page.tsx (createOrder→Razorpay→verify→/dashboard) + DB-derived yearly-discount computation — reuse the commerce logic, restyle the cards.
Retired-route 301 map in middleware.ts (RETIRED_ROUTE_REDIRECTS) — keep for deep-link back-compat, but stop linking to the retired sources from nav/footer.

res.summary: The core platform surfaces (signals, scanner, stock, markets, stocks, watchlist, dashboard, home) are genuinely feature-dense and mostly LIVE on real data, with a shared foundation kit (Card/DataTable/StatCard/Sparkline/EmptyState/Tabs/Skeleton/Reveal/ChangeBadge) that gives broad structural consistency. The Command Center dashboard and the four horizon signal pages are the most polished: glass cards, streamed GenUI morning briefing, per-symbol sparklines, confidence meters, R:R footers, sample-gated stats, and honest empty states. However the area is held back by (a) three competing color systems — lib/tokens.ts (up #16C995 / down #F23645 / primary #2BD9BC), a legacy palette hardcoded across most cards (#05B878 / #FF5947 / #FEB113), and a third set baked into the chart — none of which match the stated "AI Trading OS" intent (#00E6A7 / #00FF9D / #FF5B7F); (b) route-level loading skeletons that don't match the real page layouts and will visually jump; (c) the chart-first stock terminal hardcoding theme="dark", breaking light mode on its single most important surface; (d) the stock terminal hiding ~18 AI panels behind one right-drawer with no grouping/search, an under-designed firehose. Charting is competent (Lightweight Charts wrapper with EMAs, volume, pattern overlay, 6 timeframes) but its toolbar is inline-styled in monospace, diverging from the app's component language. The signal detail page is solid but mixes two CSS conventions (lg-surface tokens vs legacy trading-surface/d-border/numeric classes). Overall: strong bones and real data, but visual inconsistency, skeleton/layout drift, and the stock terminal's panel overload are the biggest UX debts to resolve in the redesign.

strengths: Real data everywhere — no fake sparklines, sample-gated win rates (MIN_SAMPLE=8 in categories.ts refuses to ship small-sample numbers), honest-empty states ('No live quotes', 'Movers appear when market is live'). The no-fallback discipline is visible in the UI.

The dashboard MorningBriefing is a genuinely differentiated GenUI surface: token-by-token streaming, generated insight cards, and a today's-plan checklist built from live regime/book/signals — far beyond static tiles.

Strong foundation kit drives consistency on newer pages: Card(glass)/StatCard/DataTable/EmptyState/Tabs/Reveal/ChangeBadge/Sparkline/UsageMeter/Skeleton are reused widely with good a11y (aria-sort, aria-busy, keyboard row activation, focus rings).
One shared CategorySignalsPage powers all four horizon pages — DRY, so a redesign touches one component to restyle every horizon.
SignalCard is a textbook data-dense trading card: ticker, direction, mini-chart, confidence meter, Entry/Stop/Target tiles, R:R + expected move, all in one tap target.
Genuinely good empty/CTA states (signals, watchlist, dashboard positions) that route to Copilot or paper trading rather than dead-ending.
Watchlist SkelCard and dashboard PositionSkeleton match their real layouts — the right pattern (just not applied at the route level).
Markets/Scanner embedded-agent pattern (EmbeddedAgent + ChipRow/ArtifactCard/Gauge/ActionRow) gives a consistent 'AI-in-context' affordance across research surfaces.

issues.title: Three+ competing color systems; tokens don't match design intent
issues.detail: lib/tokens.ts defines up #16C995 / down #F23645 / primary #2BD9BC / main #0B0E15. The legacy palette #05B878 (green) / #FF5947 (red) / #FEB113 (amber) is hardcoded across WatchCard, stocks/page.tsx, AIDossierPanel, ChartVisionCard, IndicatorInterpreterCard, VolumeIntelCard, RelativeStrengthCard, ProbabilityCard, SectorRotationCard, DebateTranscript. LightweightChart bakes in a 3rd set (up #05B878, blue #3D80FF, bg #0A0D14). NONE match the stated AI Trading OS intent (#00E6A7 / #00FF9D bull / #FF5B7F bear / #3B82F6 / #080B12 bg). So 'green' is literally a different green on the dashboard vs the watchlist vs the chart. The redesign must collapse these to one token set.
