But the real pages render `w-full p-4 md:p-6 xl:px-8` with KPI grids + bento/card grids (and AppShell). Result: a visible layout jump (different max-width, padding, and structure) when the real content hydrates. The in-component skeletons (SkelCard, CardSkeletons, PositionSkeleton) are correct — the route-level ones are stale.

issues.title: Stock terminal hardcodes theme="dark" on its primary chart
issues.detail: app/stock/[symbol]/page.tsx line 384 passes theme="dark" to the LightweightChart unconditionally, while the signal detail page (signals/[id]) correctly derives chartTheme from next-themes useTheme(). On the single most chart-centric page, light mode shows a dark chart slab against a light shell. Inconsistent and a clear bug for the dark+light theme requirement.

issues.title: Stock terminal AI drawer is an ungrouped ~18-panel firehose
issues.detail: The right Sheet stacks ~18 ErrorBoundary-wrapped cards (Analysis agent, Fusion, Dossier, RelStrength, VolIntel, Indicator, Probability, WhyMoving, Earnings, ChartVision, News×2, Fundamentals, VolProfile, MarketProfile, Footprint, OrderBook, Key levels) in one vertical scroll with no sections, tabs, search, or collapse. Finding anything means scrolling a wall. High cognitive load; the redesign should group these (e.g. Verdict / Technicals / Flow / News / Fundamentals) or make them tabs/accordion.

issues.title: Two CSS conventions coexist (token classes vs legacy)
issues.detail: Newer pages use the token system (bg-wrap, border-line, text-d-text-*, lg-surface, MONO). Older code uses legacy classes: trading-surface, d-border/d-border-hover, .numeric, text-white, bg-white/[0.03], text-black on buttons. Signal detail page and WatchCard mix both within one view. This makes restyling inconsistent and means the theme token swap won't fully propagate.

issues.title: Foundation DataTable not used on /stocks and /watchlist
issues.detail: DataTable (sortable, sticky, a11y, skeletons) is the canonical dense-list primitive and is used on dashboard + scanner. But /stocks hand-rolls a custom CSS-grid list + bespoke SortMenu + Pagination, and watchlist uses a card grid. Result: divergent sort/hover/keyboard/loading behavior for the two highest-traffic browse surfaces. Consolidating onto DataTable (or a shared list primitive) would unify ergonomics.

issues.title: Per-card sparkline fetch storm on signal lists
issues.detail: SignalCard's SignalSparkline SWR-fetches 30d history per symbol when no series is passed (CategorySignalsPage doesn't pass series for live data, only demo does). A page with 20+ open signals fires 20+ history requests on render (deduped only by identical symbol). Functional but a perf/UX cost (staggered chart pop-in). Consider a batch history endpoint or passing series from the list fetch.

issues.title: LightweightChart toolbar is inline-styled, off-system
issues.detail: The chart header (symbol/price + timeframe + EMA + pattern toggles) is built with raw inline style objects in monospace and its own hex PALETTE, not the foundation Button/Badge/token classes. It reads as a different app than the surrounding glass UI. The redesign should re-skin the toolbar with the component kit (the chart canvas itself can keep its theme palette).

issues.title: SectorHeatmap is a raw 8-col table, not a heatmap card
issues.detail: Despite the name, it's an HTML <table> inside <details> with opacity-stepped cell backgrounds — visually plain and dense, no real heat grid, diverges from the bento/card aesthetic. On Markets a separate, nicer 3×3 sector grid exists inline, so the app has two different sector-rotation visuals. Consolidate + upgrade to a true treemap/heat grid.

issues.title: Signal detail uses useState/useEffect instead of SWR

No background refresh, no cache reuse on back-nav, more boilerplate. Lower-priority but a consistency/maintainability gap.

issues.title: Signals hub comment/copy says '3 categories' but renders 4

issues.title: Markets page is a dense wall of inline-ternary JSX
issues.detail: app/markets/page.tsx packs ~10 research tiles with heavy inline conditional styling and single-line components — hard to maintain and the visual hierarchy is flat (every tile is an equal glass card). No clear primary/secondary emphasis for a 'morning research desk'. Readability/IA opportunity rather than a bug.

redesign_implications: Single source of truth for color is the #1 prerequisite: reconcile lib/tokens.ts to the AI Trading OS palette (or formally adopt the current tokens), then sweep out every hardcoded #05B878/#FF5947/#FEB113/#242838 in WatchCard, stocks page, and the stock cards. The check_legacy_branding/no-hex hooks mentioned in memory clearly aren't catching these — extend them to the target surfaces.

Backend contracts are stable and must be preserved: getToday/getHistory/getMomentum signal shapes, normalize()/categoryOf()/computeStats() helpers, managed.overview(), dashboard.getOverview(), watchlist.live() (with cap/capped/tier/items.engines), screener.* (powerConfluence/nlScan/runScan/sectorHeatmap/getLivePrices), publicTrust.regimeHistory/indices, dossier.get(). The redesign is a skin + IA change, not a data change.

Re-author route-level loading.tsx files to mirror the real AppShell + KPI-grid + card-grid layouts (copy the in-component skeleton patterns) so there's no hydration jump. This is cheap and high-perceived-quality.

The stock terminal is the biggest single redesign target: fix theme propagation, and restructure the 18-panel drawer into grouped/tabbed sections (Verdict · Technicals · Flow · News · Fundamentals) with the most-valuable panels (Fusion verdict, Analysis agent, key levels) pinned/visible-first.
Consolidate dense lists onto the foundation DataTable (stocks, scanner results) so sort/hover/keyboard/skeleton behavior is uniform — gives the redesign one list component to style instead of three.

Decide the chart's visual relationship to the app: keep Lightweight Charts canvas but re-skin its toolbar with the component kit + tokens so the chart stops looking like a bolted-on widget. Wire chartTheme everywhere from useTheme().
Establish a visual hierarchy on the research-heavy pages (Markets especially): not every tile should be an equal glass card. Use size/emphasis to lead the eye (regime + smart money + mood first).

Preserve the brand firewall and AI accent discipline already in place: only public engine names (Alpha/Mood/Regime/AutoPilot/Counterpoint) appear in UI, AI=purple #8B5CF6 is reserved for AI surfaces — the redesign must keep both.
reusable_assets: Foundation kit (components/foundation/*): Card(glass), DataTable, StatCard, ChangeBadge, Sparkline, Spark, EmptyState, Tabs, Skeleton, Reveal, UsageMeter, Sheet, Badge, Button, Toast — already the de-facto design language; the redesign should restyle these in place, not rebuild.

CategorySignalsPage + categories.ts + SignalCard: one component restyle re-skins all four horizon pages; the data helpers (normalize/categoryOf/isOpen/isClosed/computeStats, MIN_SAMPLE gating) are pure and reusable.
LightweightChart wrapper: keep as the single charting primitive (candles/volume/EMA/pattern overlay/timeframes) — only the toolbar chrome + theme wiring need work.
EmbeddedAgent + copilot artifacts (ChipRow, ArtifactCard, Gauge, StatPills, ActionRow): the reusable 'AI-in-context' GenUI pattern used on scanner/markets/stock — extend rather than reinvent.
MorningBriefing streaming pattern (dashboard) + StreamText tok-based renderer: a strong reusable template for any AI narration surface.
lib/tokens.ts (MONO, AI, token map) + lib/stock-href + lib/api typed client + ModelBadge + ErrorBoundary: cross-cutting utilities the redesign should standardize on.
WatchlistDigestCard / MarketExplainerCard / AiTopPicks / StockMoodLookup: deterministic-first, AI-on-demand cards that already embody the cost-aware AI pattern and can be reused across surfaces.

res.summary: The AI & trading surfaces split cleanly into two design generations that coexist in the live app. The "new" generation (copilot, portfolio, trades, strategies library/builder/deployed, fno hub container, inbox, risk) is genuinely good: it uses the `lg-surface`/glass system, `Card variant="glass"`, the `Reveal` stagger animation, foundation components (PageHeader/Badge/EmptyState/StatCard/DataTable), CSS-variable tokens in charts so themes work, and disciplined brand-firewall + honest-empty states. The Main Chat copilot is the strongest AI surface — real SSE token streaming, GenUI artifact cards (sparkline/bars/stats), a "Consulted" tool trace that maps internal tools to public labels, an honest "thinking" trace, and a polished marketing home. The "old" generation (autopilot, regime, models, paper-trading, portfolio/doctor, settings, referrals) is the weak half: it still uses the legacy `trading-surface` class, dozens of hardcoded hex literals (`#05B878`/`#FF5947`/`#4FECCD`/`#FFFFFF`) that neither match the token palette nor theme-switch, `bg-primary text-black` buttons, hand-rolled toggles/modals, and native `confirm()` dialogs. 18 files use `lg-surface` and 18 still use `trading-surface` — a near-perfect 50/50 split that makes the app feel like two products. The autopilot control UX is functionally complete and safety-conscious (mode banner, kill switch, sticky mobile stop, typed-name live-deploy friction) but visually off-brand and hardcoded. The biggest content problems: `/regime` and `/models` (public trust surfaces) describe a stale "5 AI voter" ensemble (Forecast/Gate/Alpha/Mood/Regime) that memory says was cut to 4 voters with Mood now standalone; `/engines` + `/engines/[slug]` are essentially dead stubs ("Plan 3 wires this") that contradict the live 3-engine reality; and `fo-strategies` is a 2,543-line single-file monolith. The whole-app "Ask AI" button does a hard `window.location.href` reload instead of client routing.

Uses raw Tailwind palette (purple-500/cyan-400/rose-500/text-white/glass-card) — off both token system AND AI-OS palette. No glass/Reveal polish; utilitarian.

strengths: Main Chat copilot is a genuinely strong AI surface: real SSE token streaming with graceful non-streaming fallback, GenUI artifact cards rendered from real tool data, an honest 'Consulted' tool trace mapped to brand-safe labels, a stepwise 'thinking' trace, follow-up chips, and a polished marketing home — this is the template the redesign should build the whole AI experience around.

Strong safety/friction UX on money-moving actions: AutoPilot go-live ConfirmDialog + kill-switch Dialog + mobile sticky-stop FAB; strategy paper→live requires typed-name confirmation + ack checkbox + naked-short risk detection. This is best-in-class for a retail trading product and MUST be preserved.

The 'new generation' pages (portfolio, deployed strategies, trades, strategies, inbox, fno hub, risk) are well-built: glass cards, Reveal stagger, foundation components, SWR with keepPreviousData, honest-empty states, and charts wired to CSS-variable tokens so they actually theme-switch.

Disciplined cost/honesty patterns throughout the AI surfaces: deterministic-first cards that cost 0 LLM tokens on load with an explicit user-triggered 'Get AI narrative/review' button, and honest-empty rendering (return null) when there is no real data — consistent with the no-fabricated-data rule.
BacktestViewer's 'You would have made ₹X (~Y%/yr)' hero translates a Sharpe/equity-curve into the one number a retail user cares about — excellent information design worth keeping.

Brand-firewall is mostly enforced in the live AI surfaces: copilot TOOL_LABEL map, DSLPreview EngineBadge, ModelAccuracyCard IDENTITY map, Portfolio Doctor publicLabel('cot_agents') all translate internal names to public engine labels.

issues.title: Two coexisting design generations split the app ~50/50
issues.detail: 18 files use the new lg-surface/glass + Card variant='glass' + Reveal + tokenized-chart system; 18 still use the legacy 'trading-surface' class + hardcoded hex + 'bg-primary text-black' buttons. New: copilot, portfolio, trades, strategies, deployed, fno hub, inbox, risk. Old: autopilot, regime, models, paper-trading, portfolio/doctor, settings, referrals. The result is two visibly different products inside one app — the single biggest UX-consistency problem for the redesign.

issues.title: Pervasive hardcoded hex that ignores tokens and breaks light theme
issues.detail: Legacy pages hardcode #05B878/#FF5947/#4FECCD/#FEB113/#FFFFFF/#8e8e8e directly in style props and recharts. These don't match the token palette (tokens.up=#16C995, down=#F23645, primary=#2BD9BC) and don't theme-switch — colorForScore() in Doctor, all the accent props in autopilot/paper/referrals, and BacktestViewer's recharts stroke/grid. Default text color #FFFFFF will be invisible on light backgrounds. Referrals even uses raw hex in a Tailwind class (from-[#0E1220]) which the no-raw-hex lint hook is supposed to block.

issues.title: Public /regime and /models pages describe a stale '5 AI voter' ensemble
issues.detail: /regime explainer + weight table claim 'Regime is one of five AI voters... Forecast, Gate, Alpha, Mood, Regime' with '3 of 5 / 4 of 5' agreement; /models guessStatus() docstring repeats the same 5-voter PROD set. Memory says the ensemble was cut to 4 voters (lgbm/tft/qlib/hmm, 3-of-4) and Mood is now standalone. These are user-facing TRUST surfaces presenting a factually outdated description of how the AI works.

issues.title: /engines and /engines/[slug] are dead stubs that contradict the live engine model
issues.detail: engines/[slug] is entirely Skeleton placeholders with 'Plan 3 wires this to MLflow' copy — no real accuracy chart, predictions, or retrain history. The overview comment claims '8 ML/DL engines' while lib/engines.ts has 4. It renders engine.model. It's a half-built transparency surface that should either be finished or removed in the redesign.

issues.title: Brand-firewall leak risk in /models for unmapped models
issues.detail: models/page.tsx guessStatus() and its comment hardcode internal names (regime_hmm, tft_swing, lgbm_signal_gate, qlib_alpha158, finbert_india) and ModelAccuracyCard falls back to label: props.modelName for any model not in its IDENTITY map — so a new/renamed backend model row would render its raw internal key (e.g. 'lgbm_signal_gate') as the public label. lib/models.ts public registry also still uses deprecated names (Forecast/Alpha/Gate/Mood) inconsistent with the locked public set (Alpha/Mood/Regime).

issues.title: fo-strategies is a 2,543-line single-file monolith
issues.detail: One file holds the option chain, smile/term/vol-cone charts, leg builder, AI-suggest modal, backtest modal, and adjustment logic — 38 hardcoded hex, 17 any-casts. It's the deepest F&O workspace but a severe maintainability and redesign hazard; should be decomposed into per-panel components.

issues.title: 'Ask AI' does a full-page hard reload instead of client navigation
issues.detail: dispatchCopilotOpen() uses window.location.href = '/copilot?q=...' (11 consumers across portfolio/trades/strategies/fno/inbox). Every 'Ask AI' button triggers a full document reload and re-fetch rather than a smooth router.push — a jarring transition that undercuts the otherwise-polished copilot.

issues.title: Native confirm() dialogs on destructive actions
issues.detail: Archive strategy (strategies list + mine/[id]) and rotate referral code use browser confirm() — inconsistent with the app's own ConfirmDialog/Dialog used elsewhere (autopilot kill, settings kill, live-deploy). Native confirm is unstyled, accepts on stray Enter, and breaks the design language.

issues.title: Two parallel semantic color vocabularies (up/down vs success/danger)
issues.detail: Most pages use text-up/text-down/bg-down; the risk page and StrategyCompareCard use text-success/text-danger/bg-danger. Both resolve to tokens but it's two naming systems for the same semantics — fragments the design system and confuses future contributors.

issues.title: Three separate ad-hoc markdown renderers
issues.detail: copilot renderRich(), WeeklyReviewCard Markdown(), and bold()/inline() helpers each reimplement bullet/heading/bold/number-highlight parsing. Duplicated, drift-prone logic that should be one shared component for AI prose.

issues.title: Silent error-swallowing hides failures as empty states
issues.detail: trades/page.tsx (.catch(()=>setTrades([]))), StrategyCompareCard (catch{setResult(null)}), and several others treat fetch failures identically to genuine no-data — so an outage looks like 'No trades yet' / nothing happens with no retry affordance. Honesty/UX gap.

issues.title: Settings is a 1,138-line monolith with duplicated hand-rolled toggles
issues.detail: The same toggle-switch markup (w-12 h-6 rounded-full + translate-x-6 thumb) is copy-pasted 6+ times; the Angel One credentials modal and the trading-mode/risk panels are bespoke. No shared Toggle/Modal foundation component used. Also exposes a Trading-Mode (signal/semi/full-auto) concept that overlaps the AutoPilot paper/live mode — potential user confusion about which control governs automation.

issues.title: Inconsistent layout nesting — some pages wrap their own AppShell
issues.detail: portfolio, portfolio/doctor, trades, trades/[id], settings each import and wrap <AppShell> while (platform)/* routes get the shell from the route-group layout. This dual pattern risks double-shell or missing-shell bugs and complicates a shell redesign.

issues.title: AutoPilot tier label inconsistency
issues.detail: autopilot page header renders Badge tone='primary' reading 'Pro' while the file docstring and feature framing say Elite; copy elsewhere sells AutoPilot on Pro(Lite)+Elite. The single 'Pro' badge is ambiguous about what tier actually unlocks live AutoPilot.

issues.title: Decorative non-functional controls in GenUI artifacts
issues.detail: artifacts.tsx ChipRow renders Edit/Add/X buttons and ActionRow buttons with no onClick handlers — they look interactive in embedded agent cards but do nothing, which can erode trust when users click them.

redesign_implications: The redesign's single highest-leverage move is to migrate the 18 'trading-surface' legacy files onto the lg-surface/glass + foundation-component system already proven on portfolio/deployed/trades. This is mostly mechanical (swap trading-surface→Card variant='glass', hardcoded hex→tokens, bg-primary text-black→Button) and would instantly unify the app's look.

Adopt the new copilot/portfolio/deployed pages as the canonical reference implementations; do NOT redesign from scratch — extract their patterns (glass Card, Reveal, StatCard/PageHeader/EmptyState, tokenized recharts, SWR keepPreviousData) into the redesign's component contract.

All money-moving safety UX must be preserved verbatim: AutoPilot ConfirmDialog go-live, kill-switch Dialog, mobile AutopilotStickyStop, and the strategy live-deploy typed-name + ack + naked-short modal. The redesign can re-skin them but must not reduce the friction.

Charts must use CSS-variable tokens (var(--color-up/down/line/d-text-muted)) like portfolio/page.tsx — NOT hardcoded hex like BacktestViewer/fo-strategies — or the new light theme will break. Make this a redesign lint gate.

Content audit is required alongside the visual redesign: /regime and /models must be rewritten to describe the CURRENT 4-voter ensemble (Mood standalone), and /engines must be either finished with real data or cut. These are public trust surfaces where stale '5-voter' copy is a credibility risk.

Harden the brand-firewall before redesign ships: make ModelAccuracyCard NEVER render a raw modelName fallback (show a generic label or hide), and reconcile lib/models.ts public names to the locked set (Alpha/Mood/Regime). Audit /models guessStatus() out of the user bundle.

Settings and fo-strategies are the two biggest decomposition targets — extract a shared Toggle, reuse the Dialog foundation for all modals, and split fo-strategies into per-panel components before re-skinning, or the monolith will fight every change.

Replace dispatchCopilotOpen's window.location.href with router.push so 'Ask AI' transitions are smooth client-side navigations — important because that button is the primary entry into the strongest surface.
Standardize on ONE semantic color vocabulary (recommend up/down for P&L, success/warning/danger for status) and one markdown renderer for AI prose; both currently fork.

Normalize layout nesting — every authenticated page should get its shell from the route-group layout, not wrap its own AppShell; resolve this before the shell IA (header-less desktop, dual-mode) redesign.
reusable_assets: app/(platform)/copilot/page.tsx — streaming choreography (send() with onMeta/onToken/onDone/onSaved + fallback), ArtifactCard/ChatArtifacts, ThinkingTrace, TOOL_LABEL brand-safe mapping, FOLLOWUPS. Reuse as the AI-experience reference.

components/copilot/EmbeddedAgent.tsx + artifacts.tsx — the embedded inline-agent engine and GenUI primitives (ChipRow/ArtifactCard/Bars/StatPills/Gauge/ActionRow), already theme-aware. Reuse for every feature-page agent.

components/foundation/* (PageHeader, Card variant='glass', Badge, Button variant='ai'/'secondary'/'ghost', EmptyState, StatCard, DataTable, ChangeBadge, Reveal, Skeleton, Dialog, ConfirmDialog, toast) — the canonical kit the new pages already lean on.
lib/tokens.ts — the single source of color/spacing/radius tokens; the redesign palette should be applied here (and globals.css CSS vars) so token-swap re-themes the whole app.
app/portfolio/page.tsx and app/(platform)/strategies/deployed/page.tsx — reference implementations for tokenized recharts, SWR live refresh, glass KPI strips, and per-row P&L cells.

Safety/friction components: ConfirmDialog (autopilot/settings), the live-deploy modal pattern in strategies/mine/[id] (typed-name + ack + naked-short detection), AutopilotStickyStop — reuse, don't rebuild.

Brand-firewall mappers: copilot TOOL_LABEL, DSLPreview EngineBadge, ModelAccuracyCard IDENTITY, lib/models.ts publicLabel — consolidate into one public-name layer and reuse everywhere a model/tool is named.

Deterministic-first AI card pattern (TradeReviewCard/WeeklyReviewCard/BacktestAIRead/OptionsCopilotCard): load 0-token deterministic content, gate the LLM narrative behind an explicit button, render null when empty. Reuse this contract for all embedded AI.

res.summary: The frontend↔backend contract is centralized and disciplined: a single 3,611-line `frontend/lib/api.ts` exposes a typed `api` object with ~262 typed methods spanning ~241 distinct backend `/api/*` paths (FastAPI), all flowing through one `request<T>()` helper that handles base URL, auth, query building, JSON parsing, and a structured `ApiError`. Auth is Supabase JWT (Bearer) attached per-call by `getAuthToken()`; there is NO direct Supabase DB access from app code (only auth + a single `user_profiles` read/upsert in `lib/supabase.ts`) — everything goes through the backend. Data fetching is SWR (36 `useSWR` callsites with a global `SWRConfig` in `providers.tsx`) plus 119 files importing the api client for imperative mutations; zustand is installed but UNUSED in app code. Client-side state is React Context (Auth, UiMode) + a set of small sessionStorage/localStorage caches and telemetry helpers — no global store. Tier gating is split: a backend-driven `useTier()` SWR hook (features map + caps) feeds soft gates, while hard gates surface as `ApiError` with `status===402` and a structured `detail` ({current_tier, required_tier, feature, upgrade_url}). The contract is well-typed but the type fidelity is uneven — many endpoints return `Record<string, any>` and the inline response types in api.ts are the de-facto schema (not shared with backend, so drift is possible). Real-time uses a custom `useWebSocket` (subprotocol-auth `/ws`) and one SSE stream (Copilot chat + patterns scan). This whole layer is the "backend untouched" contract the redesign must preserve verbatim.

inventory.name: api client (api object)
inventory.notes: THE contract. ~262 typed methods over ~241 /api/* paths. Namespaces: user, managed, dashboard, assistant, signals, trades, positions, portfolio, notifications, watchlist, market, broker, publicTrust, ai, autoTrader, foStrategies, earnings, dossier, portfolioDoctor, onboarding, telegram, whatsapp, weeklyReview, alerts, referrals, payments, marketplace, strategies, screener (largest — ~60 methods), paper, riskStatus, admin, discovery. Every method declares an inline response type — this IS the FE-side schema. Redesign MUST keep calling these exact shapes.

inventory.name: request<T>() core helper
inventory.notes: Single fetch wrapper. Reads NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL (defaults to '' = same-origin relative paths). Adds Accept/Content-Type, attaches Bearer token when auth!==false, parses JSON-or-text, throws ApiError(message,status,detail) on non-2xx. buildPath() drops null/undefined/'' query params. auth defaults to true; ~public endpoints pass auth:false.

inventory.name: ApiError + handleApiError + extractErrorMessage
inventory.notes: ApiError carries .status and .detail (StructuredDetail with current_tier/required_tier/feature/upgrade_url). handleApiError(err)→string used in 46 files for toast messages. This is the canonical error-handling convention; must be preserved.

inventory.name: getAuthToken()
inventory.notes: Resolves Supabase session access_token; falls back to localStorage 'sb-access-token'/'supabase.auth.token'. Every authed call depends on this. Token never goes in URL.

inventory.name: useTier() hook
inventory.notes: SWR-cached GET /api/user/tier. Returns tier/isAdmin/features(map)/copilotDailyCap + convenience predicates (isFree/isPro/isElite/isPaid; admins always pass). dedupingInterval 60s, no revalidateOnFocus/Stale. Single source of truth for client-side soft tier gating.

inventory.name: useBrokerStatus() hook
inventory.notes: SWR GET /api/broker/connections. Returns isConnected/brokerName/accountId. Gates broker-required features behind <BrokerLock>. dedupingInterval 30s.

inventory.name: useWebSocket + usePriceUpdates/useSignalUpdates/usePositionUpdates
inventory.notes: Custom WS hook. Connects to NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL (default ws://localhost:8000/ws), passes JWT via Sec-WebSocket-Protocol subprotocol ['access_token', token] (NOT in URL). Auto-reconnect (5x/3s), TOKEN_REFRESHED→clean reconnect, SIGNED_OUT→drop. usePriceUpdates batches to state every 500ms. Channel map prices→price, positions→portfolio. Message types: price_update/signal_new/position_update/notification.

inventory.name: supabase client
inventory.notes: createClient with persistSession/autoRefreshToken/detectSessionInUrl. isSupabaseConfigured guard + placeholder URL for SSG. ONLY direct DB use: getUserProfile (user_profiles read) + createUserProfile (upsert). auth.resetPassword helper. Everything else routes through backend api.

inventory.name: AuthContext / useAuth
inventory.notes: Global auth state (user/profile/loading). signUp routes through backend POST /api/auth/signup (email_confirm=True) then signInWithPassword. Mirrors Supabase session into sb-<ref>-auth-token cookie so middleware passes (backend still verifies Bearer JWT, cookie value unused). MFA AAL2 gate on signIn→/login/mfa. createUserProfile auto-creates on first load.

inventory.name: UiModeContext / useUiMode
inventory.notes: Dual-mode managed|pro. Source of truth = user_profiles.ui_preferences.ui_mode via api.user.getUIPreferences/updateUIPreferences. localStorage 'quantx.ui_mode' mirror for flicker-free paint. setMode does read-merge-write of whole prefs doc.

inventory.name: ClientAuthGate
inventory.notes: Client redirect to /login for PROTECTED_PREFIXES when no session. 600ms NAV_GRACE_MS to avoid login→dashboard race. NEXT_PUBLIC_DISABLE_AUTH dev bypass (double-guarded, non-prod only). Defence-in-depth alongside middleware.

inventory.name: middleware.ts
inventory.notes: Route protection by cookie-name presence + 28 RETIRED_ROUTE_REDIRECTS (301s, e.g. /screener→/scanner, /auto-trader→/autopilot, /assistant→/copilot, /marketplace→/strategies). publicPaths + PUBLIC_PREFIXES (/models,/track-record,/regime,/markets). Emits CSP nonce (drops unsafe-inline). Redesign must honor these redirects or break deep-links/SEO.

inventory.name: types/index.ts
inventory.notes: ~880 lines of global domain types: Signal (with legacy DB-name score fields catboost_score/tft_score/stockformer_score + ModelPredictions), Position, Trade, UserProfile, UserStats, ManagedOverview, DashboardOverview, StrategyCatalog/Deployment/Backtest, WebSocketMessage, etc. Note: TWO conflicting SubscriptionTier defs (line 59 'free|starter|pro' vs lib/hooks 'free|pro|elite').

inventory.name: types/strategies.ts
inventory.notes: DSL types mirroring backend Pydantic: EngineName='Alpha'|'Mood'|'Regime' ONLY (Pulse/Vision/Verdict/Horizon removed), Condition tree, DSLStrategy, UserStrategy, CatalogTemplate/Sections, DSLBacktestResult, StudioCompileResult, SchemaEnums. Load-bearing for Strategy Builder/Studio.

inventory.name: types/admin.ts
inventory.notes: Admin console types: SystemHealth, PaymentStats, SignalStats, UserListItem/Response, UserDetailResponse, AdminRole.

inventory.name: lib/engines.ts
inventory.notes: Public engine registry (brand firewall): Alpha/Mood/Regime/AutoPilot ONLY (4 entries; Counterpoint referenced in copy but NOT in this list). Model-agnostic descriptions, honest qualifiers. getEngineBySlug(). Real model names never ship.

inventory.name: lib/models.ts
inventory.notes: PublicModel naming registry mirroring backend/core/public_models.py. 10 keys incl. PatternScope/InsightAI/Counterpoint. publicLabel()/publicModel()/allPublicModels(). Has hardcoded hex colors that PREDATE the AI Trading OS palette — out of sync with lib/tokens.ts (redesign should reconcile or stop using these hexes).

inventory.name: lib/tradePlan.ts
inventory.notes: PURE deterministic position-sizing math (no React/IO). planTrade(): fixed-fractional risk sizing, direction inference (stop<entry=long), 1R/2R/3R targets, rMultipleToTarget, drawdown-at-risk framing. Single source of truth — AI only narrates, never changes these numbers. Critical client-side business logic to PRESERVE verbatim.

inventory.name: lib/tierUpsell.ts
inventory.notes: Shared upsell copy for /pricing + /settings: tierRank, tierAccent (hardcoded hex incl. #4FECCD that predates token palette), quizRecCopy, quizRecReason (risk-profile-aware), QUIZ_REC_DELTA feature_led|outcome_led A/B bullet sets, quizRecDelta().

inventory.name: lib/abVariant.ts
inventory.notes: Client A/B: getVariant() FNV-1a 50/50 split keyed on userId or anon localStorage id. reportExposure() POSTs /api/telemetry/experiment-exposed once per (experiment,variant) per tab session (sessionStorage dedup) via sendBeacon/fetch keepalive.

inventory.name: lib/reportUpgradeIntent.ts
inventory.notes: Fires UPGRADE_INITIATED → POST /api/telemetry/upgrade-intent (allowlisted target_tier+source). Invalidates onboarding cache. Fire-and-forget beacon. Source slugs enumerated (pricing_page, copilot_quota_modal, tier_gate_block, signal_lock, quiz_rec_* etc.).

inventory.name: lib/reportError.ts
inventory.notes: Crash telemetry → POST /api/client-errors via sendBeacon. Used by all error boundaries (global-error, route error.tsx, widget ErrorBoundary). Truncates stack/message. Never throws.

inventory.name: lib/onboardingStatusCache.ts
inventory.notes: In-memory (no storage) 5-min TTL cache + inflight dedup for GET /api/onboarding/status (read by /pricing, /settings, result page). invalidateOnboardingStatus() on tier change.

inventory.name: lib/watchlistPresetMemory.ts
inventory.notes: sessionStorage alert-preset memory (global + per-symbol) + backend cross-device sync via ui_preferences.watchlist_preset_pins (debounced 800ms read-merge-write, fire-and-forget hydrate). AlertPresetId enum.

inventory.name: lib/turnoverDismiss.ts / quizRecDismiss.ts
inventory.notes: Per-session (sessionStorage) dismiss flags for the regime-turnover warning + quiz-rec banner (keyed by recommended tier). Small UX-state utilities.

inventory.name: lib/utils.ts (cn, formatPercent, asPercent)
inventory.notes: cn() = clsx+tailwind-merge (used everywhere). formatPercent/asPercent smart-detect 0-1 ratio vs 0-100 scale (DSL backtester returns ratios; strategy_catalog stores percent) — load-bearing display logic.

inventory.name: lib/stock-href.ts
inventory.notes: stockHref() builds /stock/[symbol] safely (strips .NS/.BO, ^ index marker, encodeURIComponent for &/-/. in NSE symbols). Single source — prevents broken links.

inventory.name: lib/logger.ts
inventory.notes: Dev-only console wrapper (no-ops in production).

inventory.name: lib/tokens.ts
inventory.notes: Canonical design tokens (color/spacing/radius/type/motion/z). NOTE: token values (primary #2BD9BC, main #0B0E15) DIVERGE from the stated AI Trading OS intent (#00E6A7 / #080B12) — this file is the actual runtime truth, the spec palette is aspirational. AI=#8B5CF6, MONO helper. Redesign must reconcile spec vs this file.

inventory.name: SWRConfig (global)
inventory.notes: Global SWR defaults: revalidateOnFocus false, dedupingInterval 5000ms, errorRetryCount 3, errorRetryInterval 5000, shouldRetryOnError true. Wraps AuthProvider+UiModeProvider+ClientAuthGate. next-themes (defaultTheme light, storageKey quantx.theme), sonner Toaster (theme-reactive).

inventory.name: zustand
inventory.notes: Installed (^4.5.0) but ZERO usage in app code (grep found only node_modules). All client state is React Context + SWR + storage utils. Redesign can drop the dep or finally use it.

inventory.name: raw-fetch bypass endpoints
inventory.notes: Network calls NOT through api client: AuthContext→/api/auth/signup; (platform)/layout.tsx→/api/push/vapid-key + /api/push/subscribe (web push); admin/layout.tsx→/api/admin/verify; reportError→/api/client-errors; reportUpgradeIntent→/api/telemetry/upgrade-intent; abVariant→/api/telemetry/experiment-exposed; PatternsV2Tab→EventSource /api/screener/patterns/v2/scan/stream; useWebSocket→/ws. Redesign must keep these too.

strengths: Single, exhaustively-typed API client (frontend/lib/api.ts) is the entire backend contract in one file — every response shape is declared inline, making the FE→BE dependency map fully enumerable and the 'backend untouched' boundary explicit and trivially auditable.

One disciplined network path: every authed call funnels through request<T>() with consistent Bearer-token attachment, query sanitization (drops null/undefined/''), JSON/text parsing, and a structured ApiError carrying status + tier-gate detail. Error handling convention (handleApiError→toast) is uniform across 46 files.

Strong separation of concerns: NO direct Supabase DB reads from app/components (only auth + one user_profiles helper). Backend is the sole data authority — redesign can swap UI freely without touching data access.

Tier gating is cleanly bifurcated: soft gates from the cached useTier() features map (one network call, deduped) and hard gates as 402 ApiError with machine-readable detail (current_tier/required_tier/feature/upgrade_url) — UI branches without regexing messages.

tradePlan.ts and utils.formatPercent encode load-bearing, well-documented deterministic business logic (position sizing, ratio-vs-percent scale detection) that is pure and reusable — the redesign inherits correct math for free.
Real-time auth is done right: WS token passed via subprotocol (never URL), with TOKEN_REFRESHED clean-reconnect and SIGNED_OUT teardown — no token leakage to logs/CDN.

Brand firewall is enforced at the data layer (engines.ts/models.ts expose only public names; internal TFT/Qlib/FinBERT/HMM names live only in DB-legacy field names like catboost_score, never in display copy).
SWR usage is consistent and centrally configured (global SWRConfig), with custom hooks (useTier, useBrokerStatus) wrapping the common reads so callers don't duplicate fetch+state logic.

issues.title: api.ts response types are FE-only and not generated from the backend — silent drift risk
issues.detail: All ~262 method response types are hand-written inline in frontend/lib/api.ts and types/*.ts, mirroring backend Pydantic models by convention only (comments say 'mirrors backend/...'). There is no codegen/OpenAPI sync. Several endpoints already encode this fragility: foStrategies comment notes a payload swap (forecast_p50_5d→mean_5d) after vix_tft was dropped; types/index.ts Signal still carries legacy DB names (catboost_score/tft_score/stockformer_score). A redesign that touches these shapes can pass tsc while being wrong at runtime.

issues.title: Pervasive Record<string,any> escape hatches erase the contract at exactly the high-traffic endpoints
issues.detail: Core surfaces return untyped blobs: market.getQuote/getOHLC/getIndices/getStatus, portfolio.getSummary/getPerformance, signals.getPerformance, screener.getTechnicals/getStockPrice/getLivePrices, trades.execute body, all admin.* stats endpoints, dossier.scores partials. The redesign gets no compile-time help on these — the real shape lives only in the consuming component or the backend. This is where breakage will be invisible.

issues.title: Duplicate / conflicting SubscriptionTier definitions
issues.detail: types/index.ts:59 defines SubscriptionTier = 'free'|'starter'|'pro' (stale — 'starter' tier no longer exists), while lib/hooks/useTier.ts + everywhere else uses 'free'|'pro'|'elite'. StrategyCatalog.tier_required and StrategyDeployment.trade_mode reference the stale type. A redesign reusing types/index.ts inherits the wrong tier enum.

issues.title: Hardcoded hex colors in data-layer files diverge from lib/tokens.ts AND from the AI Trading OS spec
issues.detail: lib/models.ts (per-model hex: #4FECCD, #FEB113, #5DCBD8...) and lib/tierUpsell.ts tierAccent (#FFD166, #4FECCD, #8E8E8E) bake in colors that predate the current palette. Worse, lib/tokens.ts itself (primary #2BD9BC, main #0B0E15) does NOT match the documented AI Trading OS intent (#00E6A7 / #080B12). Three competing color sources — the redesign must pick one and the spec palette is NOT what ships today.

issues.title: Token resolution does an async Supabase getSession() on EVERY authed request
issues.detail: request() calls getAuthToken()→supabase.auth.getSession() per call. With 36 SWR hooks + 119 imperative call sites, a busy dashboard fires many getSession() awaits. Supabase caches in-memory so it's cheap, but it adds an await to every request and there's no shared token memo at the api-client level. Minor perf/architecture smell the redesign could improve with an interceptor/token cache.

issues.title: zustand is a dead dependency
issues.detail: zustand ^4.5.0 is in package.json but has zero usage in app code (only node_modules type defs match). All state is Context + SWR + storage helpers. Either remove the dep or adopt it intentionally in the redesign; right now it's noise.

issues.title: Two parallel chat contracts (assistant vs copilot) with overlapping responsibility
issues.detail: api.assistant.chat (/api/assistant/chat, AssistantChatResponse) and api.ai.copilotChat/copilotChatStream (/api/ai/copilot/*) both exist. Memory says /assistant folded into /copilot and the standalone page is retired, but the assistant endpoints + AssistantUsage/CopilotQuotaModal machinery remain wired for back-compat. The redesign should consolidate the chat surface and decide which contract is canonical.

issues.title: Quota/cap signaling is inconsistent across endpoints (402 vs 429 vs in-body flags)
issues.detail: Watchlist cap → ApiError 402 with detail; Copilot quota → 429 + dispatched CustomEvent('copilot:quota_exhausted'); several endpoints return in-body flags instead (tier_cap_applied/tier_cap on signals.today, llm_capped on watchlist.digest/news-intelligence, capped on watchlist.live). Three different conventions the UI must each special-case. Redesign would benefit from one cap-signaling pattern.

issues.title: Screener namespace is a 60+ method grab-bag mixing concerns
issues.detail: api.screener holds everything from PK scanners to F&O snapshots, order-flow, fundamentals, news-intelligence, market-depth, verdict, why-moving, market-profile — far beyond 'screening'. It's the largest namespace and hard to navigate. Not a contract bug, but the redesign's IA should not assume screener==one feature; it spans many surfaces.

redesign_implications: BACKEND IS UNTOUCHED = api.ts is frozen contract. The redesign re-skins components but MUST keep importing the same `api.*` methods with the same arguments and consuming the same response shapes. Treat frontend/lib/api.ts (all 262 methods / 241 paths) + types/index.ts + types/strategies.ts as the immutable interface; do not 'clean up' shapes without coordinated backend changes.

Every authed page depends on AuthContext (Supabase session→Bearer) AND the cookie-mirror bridge for middleware. A new shell must keep AuthProvider mounted above all data fetching and preserve the sb-<ref>-auth-token cookie write, or full-navigation routing breaks (login→dashboard bounce loop).

Tier gating logic is split between useTier() (soft, features map) and 402 ApiError detail (hard). The redesign's lock/upsell components must consume BOTH: render soft gates from useTier().features and catch ApiError status 402/429 to show upgrade UI. Reuse the existing CopilotQuotaModal event pattern + reportUpgradeIntent source slugs to keep funnel analytics intact.

Dual-mode (managed|pro) via UiModeContext drives the entire shell IA (Home·My Money·Activity·Settings vs full terminal). The new sidebar/nav MUST read useUiMode() and the source of truth is server ui_preferences.ui_mode (localStorage is only a paint mirror). Don't hardcode nav.

Color tokens are in conflict: lib/tokens.ts (what ships) vs the AI Trading OS spec (#00E6A7/#080B12, aspirational) vs hardcoded hexes in models.ts/tierUpsell.ts. The redesign must FIRST reconcile to one palette in lib/tokens.ts + globals.css CSS vars, then purge the inline hexes from models.ts/tierUpsell.ts. Do not assume the spec palette is live.

28 RETIRED_ROUTE_REDIRECTS in middleware.ts encode the v1→v2 URL migration. New routes must either match the redirect targets or update the map — otherwise notification deep-links, SEO, and bookmarks break. PUBLIC_PREFIXES (/models,/track-record,/regime,/markets) must stay crawlable (no auth gate).

Real-time surfaces (WS price/signal/position updates + 2 SSE streams) are bespoke. The redesign must keep useWebSocket's subprotocol-auth handshake and the EventSource patterns; these are not SWR and won't be picked up by a generic data-layer refactor.

Brand firewall is data-layer enforced (engines.ts/models.ts). The redesign must source ALL engine/model display names from these registries (Alpha·Mood·Regime·AutoPilot·Counterpoint) and never surface DB-legacy field names (catboost_score/tft_score/stockformer_score) as labels.

Deterministic client logic (tradePlan.ts, formatPercent/asPercent, stockHref) is pure and correct — reuse as-is. Re-implementing position-sizing math or the ratio-vs-percent scale heuristic in new components would introduce bugs.

No global store today (zustand unused). If the redesign needs cross-page client state (e.g., a persistent copilot rail, command palette), it can adopt the already-installed zustand cleanly, but most needs are already served by SWR cache + Context.

res.summary: The backend is a single FastAPI app (backend/api/app.py) that registers ~40 routers via guarded try/except include_router blocks, plus a custom register_screener_routes() and register_admin_routes(). Every router import is wrapped so a broken module degrades to a logged warning instead of crashing boot — robust, but it means a misconfigured router silently disappears from the surface. There are two prefix conventions in use: some routers carry full /api/... paths in their decorators (signals, trades, market, watchlist, etc., included with no prefix), while newer routers use a short prefix (e.g. /broker, /auto-trader, /fo-strategies) and are included with prefix="/api". Auth is a local HS256 JWT verify (get_current_user) with a Supabase network fallback; tier gating is centralized in backend/middleware/tier_gate.py (RequireTier/RequireFeature → 402 with a structured upgrade payload) but is applied inconsistently — only ~11 of ~43 route files use it, and several money/risk paths (trades/execute, strategy transition) hand-roll their own 403/422 tier and gate checks instead. The screener router alone exposes ~95 endpoints (the single largest and most sprawling surface). The contract is broad and the frontend (one giant frontend/lib/api.ts, 3610 lines) consumes ~140+ distinct paths. Known issues: a real frontend↔backend path mismatch on telemetry (frontend posts /api/telemetry/*, backend mounts /api/client-errors/*), 12 deprecated alias routes intentionally hidden from OpenAPI, and dead-feature router slots (AI SIP/F5) already removed. The strategy quality gate (evaluation.py) and global kill-switch are correctly enforced on the live-trading paths — these are load-bearing and must be preserved.

strengths: Every router is registered behind try/except include_router — one broken module degrades to a logged warning instead of crashing the whole API. Resilient boot.

Auth is solid: get_current_user does local HS256 signature+exp+aud verification (no per-request Supabase round-trip), rejects forged tokens with explicit InvalidSignatureError handling, and get_user_profile fails CLOSED (503) instead of synthesizing a Pro fallback (a real prior vuln they fixed).
WebSocket auth moved to the Sec-WebSocket-Protocol header so JWTs never leak into URLs/logs/CDN; the legacy token-in-path endpoint was deliberately deleted.

Tier gating is centralized and well-designed where used: tier_gate.py returns a structured 402 payload (required_tier/current_tier/feature/upgrade_url) the frontend keys on to show an upgrade modal, plus analytics tracking of gate hits.

The money-critical paths are correctly defended: trades/execute consults the GLOBAL kill-switch before any per-user state, and strategy transition→live enforces BOTH a tier gate (403) AND the out-of-sample quality gate (422 gate_failed) from evaluation.py.
Consistent global exception handlers normalize 422 (field-level details), HTTPException, and 500 (never leaks internals) — predictable error envelope for the frontend.
Deprecated routes are handled responsibly: kept functional for back-compat but hidden from OpenAPI (include_in_schema=False) so new clients pick canonical paths.
Sentry integration scrubs Authorization/Cookie/token/broker_token before events leave the process — good defense-in-depth.
Dashboard + managed overview are single round-trip aggregates — fewer client requests, good for redesign perf.
Brand firewall respected on public/admin surfaces (public_routes returns engine labels, not TFT/Qlib/FinBERT/HMM).

issues.title: Frontend↔backend telemetry path mismatch (silent 404s)
issues.detail: Backend mounts the telemetry router at prefix '/client-errors' → /api/client-errors/{upgrade-intent,experiment-exposed}. But frontend lib/reportUpgradeIntent.ts (ENDPOINT='/api/telemetry/upgrade-intent', line 15) and lib/abVariant.ts (ENDPOINT='/api/telemetry/experiment-exposed', line 99) post to /api/telemetry/*. Those beacons 404. Currently masked because reportUpgradeIntent has an early 'if (true) return;' short-circuit, but it's a latent contract break: the conversion-funnel + A/B-exposure analytics the code documents are not actually reaching the backend. Either rename the router prefix to /telemetry (it also serves /client-errors root for reportError) or fix the two frontend ENDPOINT constants.

issues.title: Inconsistent tier-gating enforcement across routers
issues.detail: The clean centralized tier_gate.py (RequireTier/RequireFeature) is used in only ~11 of ~43 route files (ai, earnings, fo_strategies, alerts, portfolio_doctor, referrals, auto_trader, screener, vision, weekly_review, whatsapp). Other paid/risk paths hand-roll their own checks: strategies transition uses a bespoke _LIVE_DEPLOY_TIERS 403, trades/execute uses ad-hoc subscription_status/fo_enabled/trading_mode 403s, signals uses current_user_tier for content shaping without hard gates. Functionally OK today but the gate logic is duplicated and drift-prone — a redesign touching pricing/tier rules has to find and update many call sites, not one.

issues.title: Two prefix conventions create fragile shared-prefix overlaps
issues.detail: Older routers embed full /api/... in each decorator and are included with no prefix; newer ones use a short prefix + include prefix='/api'. Three pairs now share the same effective prefix from different files: /api/strategies (strategies_routes.py AND strategy_runner_routes.py), /api/watchlist (watchlist_routes.py AND watchlist_live_routes.py), /api/portfolio (portfolio_routes.py AND portfolio_doctor_routes.py at /doctor). Route precedence depends on registration order in app.py; e.g. strategies_routes has both GET '' and GET /{strategy_id}, and strategy_runner adds /{id}/signals — a new dynamic route in the wrong file could shadow another. Works now but is a footgun for anyone adding endpoints during the redesign.

issues.title: screener_routes.py is a 140KB / ~95-endpoint monolith
issues.detail: register_screener_routes wires two routers carrying ~95 endpoints in a single file mixing scanners, AI forecasts, F&O/OI, orderflow, prices, fundamentals, news, saved-scans CRUD, and 11 hidden legacy aliases. It is by far the largest API module and the hardest to reason about for auth/tier coverage. Several endpoints (footprint, market-profile, smart-money/fii-dii, depth) are honest-empty placeholders that need a paid L2/tick feed; the redesign must know which screener cards are real vs honest-empty.

issues.title: System status route mis-tagged as Admin but open to any user
issues.detail: system_routes.py GET /api/system/status uses tags=['Admin'] and Depends(get_current_user) (any authenticated user), not the admin gate. Cosmetic OpenAPI grouping mismatch, but could mislead a redesign into thinking it's admin-only or, conversely, that admin endpoints live here.

issues.title: Router registration is silent-fail; a broken module vanishes from the API
issues.detail: All ~40 includes are try/except → logger.warning. This is good for resilience but means a typo/import error in any *_routes.py removes its whole surface at runtime with only a startup log line. No /api/health or readiness check enumerates which routers actually registered, so a partial-boot (e.g. signals router failed) is invisible to the frontend until calls 404. Consider exposing registered-router status in /ready.

issues.title: Single 3610-line frontend/lib/api.ts is the entire client contract
issues.detail: All ~140 backend paths are referenced from one monolithic api object. There is no per-domain client split, so the redesign's frontend refactor inherits one giant file that must stay byte-accurate to the backend prefixes (including the short-prefix vs full-path quirks above). This is where path-mismatch bugs like the telemetry one hide.

redesign_implications: This API catalog IS the contract — the redesign is frontend/UX only (per memory: redesigns preserve features, backend untouched). Every path listed must keep working; do not rename backend prefixes during the UI rebuild.

Tier gating semantics are load-bearing for the UI: a 402 with detail.error=='tier_gate' (+required_tier/current_tier/feature/upgrade_url) must trigger an UpgradeModal, NOT a generic error toast. Some paths instead return 403 with detail.error=='tier_required' (strategies transition) or 422 detail.error=='gate_failed' (quality gate) — the redesign's error handler must recognize all three shapes.

GET /api/user/tier is the single source the frontend uses to decide which features/CTAs to show (tier + features map + copilot_daily_cap). The redesigned shell (Managed vs Pro modes) should drive nav/feature visibility from this one call.

Dual-mode IA maps to /api/managed/overview (beginner Home, ungated, returns autopilot.available=false to drive upsell) and the richer per-feature endpoints for Pro mode — both already exist; the redesign wires UI to them, not new backend.

The header-less desktop shell + recent-chats sidebar maps to ai_routes copilot endpoints (chat/stream SSE + conversations CRUD); the redesign should consume the SSE stream + GenUI artifacts, not the JSON fallback, for the streaming chat experience.

Public engine names (Alpha/Mood/Regime/AutoPilot/Counterpoint) come through /api/market/sentiment (Mood), /api/signals/* (Alpha), /api/market/regime + /api/public/regime/history (Regime), /api/auto-trader/* (AutoPilot), /api/ai/debate/signal/{id} (Counterpoint). The brand firewall is enforced server-side on public_routes — the redesign must not surface raw model names even if a non-public endpoint leaks them.

WebSocket /ws uses the ['access_token', jwt] subprotocol handshake and channels price/signals/portfolio/notifications — any redesigned realtime UI (live prices, signal toasts) must keep that exact handshake; there is no token-in-URL fallback anymore.
Fix the telemetry path mismatch as part of the redesign's analytics wiring, or the conversion-funnel + A/B exposure data the new UI relies on will keep silently 404ing.

Be explicit in the redesign about which screener cards are real vs honest-empty (footprint, market-profile, smart-money/fii-dii, depth need a paid feed) so the new UI doesn't render dead tiles as live.

Money/risk surfaces (trades/execute, strategy transition→live, auto-trader toggle, admin kill-switch) carry server-side guards (global halt, OOS gate, role checks). The redesign must surface these states (halted, gate_failed) honestly rather than hide them, and must not add a client-side bypass.
reusable_assets: backend/middleware/tier_gate.py — RequireTier / RequireFeature / current_user_tier + the structured 402 payload. Reuse as the ONE gating primitive; the redesign should push the hand-rolled 403 checks (trades, strategies) onto this rather than inventing new ones.
backend/api/app.py get_current_user / get_user_profile / _verify_ws_token — the canonical JWT verification for REST and WebSocket. Reuse, don't reimplement, in any new endpoint.
GET /api/user/tier response shape ({tier, is_admin, features: Record<string,boolean>, copilot_daily_cap}) — the contract the redesigned shell should drive feature visibility from.
GET /api/dashboard/overview and GET /api/managed/overview — ready-made single-round-trip aggregates for the Pro and Managed home screens respectively.
Global exception envelope (422 {error,details[]}, others {error}) from app.py handlers — the stable error shape the new frontend error handler can rely on.

The 12 include_in_schema=False alias routes (11 screener short-names + /api/positions) — keep them functional for back-compat; the redesign should target the canonical paths (/api/screener/scan/{id}, /api/positions/open).
public_routes.py (regime/history, track-record, models, signal-of-the-day, indices) — already brand-firewalled, ungated trust-surface endpoints reusable for marketing/landing redesign.
ai_routes copilot/chat/stream (SSE) + build_artifacts GenUI contract — reusable streaming engine for the redesigned Main Chat + embedded agents.
frontend/lib/api.ts — the existing typed client; reuse its types/response models even if the file is split per-domain during the redesign, to preserve the exact path strings.

res.summary: The backend is a large, genuinely-engineered FastAPI/ML platform split into backend/services/* (feature business logic), backend/data/* (market data + brokers + screener), backend/ai/* (signal ensemble + LLM agents + registry), backend/trading/* (executors + AutoPilot), and ml/* (training spine). The CORE live signal brain is a 4-voter ensemble in backend/ai/signals/generator.py: Qlib Alpha158 ranker (public "Alpha", 0.20), TFT 5-day quantile forecaster (0.30, no public brand), LightGBM BUY/HOLD/SELL gate (0.30, public "Gate"), and a 3-state Gaussian HMM regime detector (public "Regime", 0.10) — with a strict no-fallback contract (SignalGenerator.__init__ raises if any artifact is missing). Sentiment ("Mood") was REMOVED from the ensemble (2026-06-06) and is now a standalone engine; critically it runs an OpenRouter LLM zero-shot classifier by default, NOT the FinBERT neural net (only loads with USE_FINBERT_FALLBACK=1). A SECOND, newer signal architecture coexists: per-style engines under backend/ai/signals/engines/ — but ONLY momentum.py exists (serves a trained momentum_lambdarank LightGBM via GET /api/signals/momentum); swing/positional/intraday engines are NOT built despite the "4-engine" framing. The LLM layer is OpenRouter-only (no LangChain): a custom GraphRunner runs Copilot chat, FinRobot Portfolio Doctor (5 agents), and Bull/Bear Counterpoint debate, plus single-shot roles (NL→DSL strategy, NL screen, chart vision, digests), all on mostly-free models under a $50/mo budget kill-switch. The data layer is split and inconsistent: the serving provider (backend/data/market.py) selects "free"(yfinance) or "kite"; the ML loader (ml/data/data_loader.py) selects "free" or "truedata" — TrueData exists but is trial-only and not wired into the serving path. docs/QUANTX_AI_SYSTEMS_EXPLAINED.md is an accurate, brutally honest audit that still holds, with one stale point: "momentum picks has no model" is now false (momentum_lambdarank is trained + serving). The honest reality the doc states stands: the models' measured edge is weak (Qlib realmoney_pass:false / IC≈0.03, LGBM Sharpe≈0.1) — these are display/ranking-grade, not proven alpha. A strong strategy-promotion gate (backend/ai/strategy/evaluation.py, OOS walk-forward) protects the one path to live money.

Frontend mirror frontend/lib/models.ts. Note: PUBLIC_MODELS uses Forecast/Intraday/Gate/PatternScope/InsightAI which differ from the memory's Alpha/Mood/Regime/AutoPilot/Counterpoint 5-engine list.

strengths: Genuine no-fallback discipline in the signal path: SignalGenerator.__init__ raises if any of LGBM/HMM/TFT/Qlib is missing rather than degrading to heuristics — the live pipeline goes dark instead of faking signals. This is real, rare, and enforced.

A clean model registry abstraction (B2 bytes + Postgres model_versions truth) with atomic promote/shadow/retire and a graceful disk fallback (compat.resolve_model_file) so dev/CI works without B2 credentials.

Strong, honest ML-eval tooling: ml/eval has SPA/deflated-Sharpe/PBO (overfitting.py, spa.py), purged/combinatorial CV (purged_cv/cpcv/wfcv), impact-cost and drift monitors — the quant rigor most retail platforms lack.

The strategy-promotion gate (backend/ai/strategy/evaluation.py + walk_forward) is the single most valuable safety asset: it scores OUT-OF-SAMPLE only, requires holdout-positive + cross-fold consistency, and is the only path to live money — directly protecting users' capital.

The LLM layer is well-architected and cost-disciplined: a custom GraphRunner (no LangChain bloat), per-agent model map, free->free->paid fallback, and a hard $50/mo budget kill-switch with persisted spend (observability/llm_budget).
The brand-firewall is real and centralized: public_models.public_label() guarantees TFT/Qlib/FinBERT/HMM never leak to UI; agents/services consistently route user-facing labels through it.

The MomentumEngine is a clean, well-documented template (registry-first load, full-panel feature build to avoid xs-rank skew, honest-empty, risk_engine.derive_levels reuse) that the swing/positional/intraday engines can copy.

The scheduler (backend/platform/scheduler.py, ~55 cron jobs) is the well-organized operational heartbeat tying every AI/data subsystem to a real cadence (qlib rank, sentiment refresh, autopilot rebalance, discovery, digests, drift).
docs/QUANTX_AI_SYSTEMS_EXPLAINED.md is an unusually honest internal audit that the code still corroborates — it is a reliable map for the redesign team (with the one momentum stale-point noted).

issues.title: The '4-engine' style program is only 1/4 built — momentum is the lone serving style engine
issues.detail: backend/ai/signals/engines/ contains only momentum.py; style_types.Style enum has only MOMENTUM (SWING/POSITIONAL/INTRADAY commented out); ml/training/trainers/ has no swing/positional/intraday style trainer. The active-build memory framing of '4 style engines on TrueData' is aspirational — the redesign must not surface swing/positional/intraday style pages as if engines exist behind them.

issues.title: Two coexisting, divergent signal architectures (legacy 4-voter ensemble vs new per-style engines)
issues.detail: The live swing path is the 4-voter SignalGenerator (LGBM+TFT+Qlib+HMM, persisted to signals table, served via /api/signals/today). The new path is MomentumEngine (cross-sectional ranker, computed live, served via /api/signals/momentum). They use different data loaders, different output schemas (GeneratedSignal vs StyleSignal), and different provider selection. The redesign must decide which is canonical or how they coexist on the Signals surface — today they are parallel and unreconciled.

issues.title: Sentiment public name 'Mood / FinBERT-India' overstates what runs: it is an LLM zero-shot classifier by default
issues.detail: SentimentEngine._select_classifier() picks LLMFinanceClassifier (OpenRouter) first; the FinBERT neural net only loads with USE_FINBERT_FALLBACK=1. The model_versions row is tagged finbert_india/'loaded_at_runtime'. Any redesign copy or model-explainer that says 'FinBERT' is technically inaccurate for the running system. Defensible engineering choice, but the label is a latent honesty/marketing risk.

issues.title: On-disk artifacts diverge from the registry's PROD records (TFT + LGBM)
issues.detail: Registry PROD tft_swing v3 describes a neuralforecast model (hidden=128); the on-disk tft_model.ckpt is a smaller pytorch_forecasting model (hidden=32) and TFTPredictor.load_from_checkpoint is ONLY compatible with the on-disk one. The live LGBM is the legacy 15-feature model (via split_feature_sets) while a 30-feature v2 (ml/features/lgbm_v2.py) is dormant. So which model actually runs depends on whether B2 is wired — the 'PROD metrics' shown may not describe the running artifact. Don't surface registry metrics (e.g. '68% accuracy') as live performance.

issues.title: lgbm_signal_gate is REQUIRED by the ensemble but is NOT is_prod in model_versions
issues.detail: SignalGenerator hard-requires lgbm_signal_gate.txt, but the doc notes it is not flagged is_prod in the DB; it loads only because compat.resolve_model_file finds the file on disk regardless of the PROD flag. This registry-vs-runtime inconsistency means a B2-only deployment that trusts is_prod could fail to resolve the gate. Tighten before relying on registry-only deploys.

issues.title: Serving vs training data-provider selection is inconsistent
issues.detail: backend/data/market.py selects DATA_PROVIDER in {'free'(yfinance),'kite'}; ml/data/data_loader.py selects {'free','truedata'} and RAISES on 'kite'. The MomentumEngine + trainers use the ml loader, the legacy ensemble uses the market provider. TrueData is trial-only and unwired into serving. A single DATA_PROVIDER env can therefore mean different things to different subsystems — a train/serve data-shape skew and ops footgun.

issues.title: Measured edge is weak across the board — display/ranking-grade, not proven alpha
issues.detail: Per the models' own metadata (corroborated by the doc): Qlib qlib_realmoney_pass:false, rank_ic_mean≈0.031; LGBM CV Sharpe≈0.097 (≈0 edge, per-fold -7.9..+5.6); HMM 'confidence' structurally ~1.0 with no validated regime accuracy. The strong engineering wraps an unproven-live edge. Redesign should avoid implying validated profitability and keep confidence/accuracy framing honest.

issues.title: Earnings predictor is presented as an engine but has no trained model
issues.detail: backend/ai/earnings/ has only calendar.py; dossier_routes/earnings_routes read a cached earnings_predictions table (calendar/rule-hydrated), and public_label('earnings_predictor') is shown to users. The doc explicitly flags 'earnings predictor has no model on disk'. Effectively rule-based — don't redesign an 'AI Earnings' surface implying a predictive model.

issues.title: PUBLIC_MODELS contains more brands than the locked 5-engine memory list
issues.detail: public_models.py defines Alpha/Mood/Regime/Forecast/Intraday/Gate/AutoPilot/PatternScope/InsightAI/Counterpoint, while the AI-Trading-OS memory locks the 5 public engines as Alpha/Mood/Regime/AutoPilot/Counterpoint. The extra keys (Forecast/Intraday/Gate/PatternScope/InsightAI) appear in reason strings/dossier. The redesign should reconcile the brand surface so users see the locked 5, not 10.

issues.title: generator.py docstring/comments are stale vs the actual pipeline
issues.detail: The file header still describes '6 backtested strategies' and the per-method docstring still lists 'FinBERT → news sentiment' as a voter, but the alpha path is model-first 4-voter with sentiment removed and the 6 strategies relegated to Scanner Lab. Misleading for anyone reading the code as documentation.

redesign_implications: Backend contracts are stable and must be preserved untouched (per the redesign-preserves-features rule). The redesign is a UI/UX skin over: GET /api/signals/today|intraday|history|performance|momentum|{id}, the ai_routes copilot/doctor/debate endpoints, portfolio_doctor, screener, vision, earnings, autopilot streams, and managed overview. Do not change service signatures.

The signal surface has TWO sources that must be reconciled in IA: the legacy persisted 4-voter swing signals (signals table) and the live-computed momentum ranker (/api/signals/momentum). Decide whether 'Alpha/Signals' shows the ensemble, the style engines, or both — and only expose style tabs (swing/positional/intraday) once those engines exist (today only momentum is real).

Brand-firewall is enforced server-side via public_label(); the redesign must continue routing every model reference through PUBLIC_MODELS and never render TFT/Qlib/FinBERT/HMM/LightGBM. Also reconcile the 10-entry PUBLIC_MODELS map down to the locked 5 user-facing engines to avoid confusing brand sprawl.

Keep all model/confidence copy honest: the platform's own metadata says signals are display/ranking-grade (Qlib realmoney_pass:false, weak Sharpe/IC, degenerate HMM confidence). The new UI should NOT imply validated live profitability; lean on the genuinely strong story instead (no-fallback discipline, enforced OOS backtest gate, budget-capped advisory AI).

'Mood' and 'Earnings' are the two surfaces most likely to overstate: Mood runs an LLM (not FinBERT) and Earnings has no model. Redesign copy/explainers for these must be carefully worded (e.g. 'AI-read news sentiment', 'earnings calendar + heuristics').

The LLM agent layer is embedded-per-page (Copilot inline + Doctor + Counterpoint debate) and standalone Main Chat with real SSE streaming. The redesign's embedded-agent GenUI cards can rely on these existing endpoints (run_copilot/run_copilot_stream, run_finrobot_doctor, debate route) and the $50 budget kill-switch — no new agent infra needed.

AutoPilot is an orchestrator, not a model; its outputs (RebalanceDecision, track_record_aggregation, drawdown breaker, streams) are the data the redesigned AutoPilot dashboard should bind to. Honor the locked copy: 'AutoPilot on your own broker account', never 'we manage your money'.

Data-source honesty: many microstructure/L2/tick/footprint surfaces are honest-empty without a paid feed, and TrueData is trial-only/unwired. The redesign must keep honest-empty states (not fake data) for these and not promise live depth where only EOD/yfinance is available.

Theme/palette work is purely frontend; nothing in this backend area constrains the AI-Trading-OS palette — but the per-engine hex_color values in public_models.py (e.g. Alpha #5DCBD8, Mood #05B878, Regime #FF9900) are an OLD palette and conflict with the new emerald-teal #00E6A7 signature. Update these to the new tokens if engine accent colors are surfaced in UI.
reusable_assets: backend/core/public_models.py public_label()/PUBLIC_MODELS — the single brand-firewall map; reuse for every model reference in the new UI (mirror frontend/lib/models.ts).
backend/ai/registry/* (ModelRegistry + compat resolver) — the model-loading contract; the redesign needs no awareness of it but it guarantees artifacts resolve identically across envs.
GET /api/signals/* contract + GeneratedSignal/MomentumSignal/StyleSignal schemas (backend/ai/signals/types.py + style_types.py) — bind signal cards directly to these.

backend/ai/agents/llm.py LLM adapter + GraphRunner + tool_registry + observability/llm_budget — the complete advisory-AI backbone (streaming, fallback, budget cap) to power embedded GenUI agents without rebuilding.

backend/ai/strategy/evaluation.py GateThresholds/evaluate_gate + walk_forward — the live-money safety gate; the Strategy Studio redesign must keep routing ->live transitions through it (422 gate_failed).
backend/trading/risk_engine.derive_levels + risk.py overlays + MomentumEngine pattern — reusable level-derivation and the canonical template for building the remaining style engines.

backend/platform/scheduler.py job graph — the authoritative list of what runs when; the redesign's 'system status'/admin surfaces should read these job IDs + model_versions + rolling-performance tables.
backend/data/providers/base.DataProvider Protocol + OHLCVRequest tidy frame + DepthBus/realtime/depth_to_ws — the unified data + live-depth contracts for any market/chart components.
backend/services/managed_overview.py + portfolio analytics/coach_flags + autopilot/track_record — deterministic, ready-to-bind data for the Managed (beginner) mode dashboards.
ml/eval/* (spa, overfitting/PBO, kelly, impact_cost, drift_monitor) — credibility assets the redesign can surface (honestly) as proof of methodology rigor in trust/marketing surfaces.

res.summary: Quant X is a large, genuinely-wired Next.js 14 + FastAPI + ML platform: 62 frontend page.tsx routes, 330 route decorators across 41 backend route modules, 5 trained ML models behind one swing-signal ensemble, a custom GraphRunner LLM layer (3 multi-agent graphs + 7 single-shot roles, all on free OpenRouter models under a $20/mo kill-switch), real Razorpay 3-tier billing, real direct-broker OAuth, and an APScheduler crew of ~40 jobs. The two reference audit docs (docs/QUANTX_FULL_FEATURE_AUDIT.md and docs/QUANTX_AI_SYSTEMS_EXPLAINED.md, dated 06-05/06-06) are thorough but PARTIALLY STALE versus current code on feat/mldl-4engine: three of their four headline "sold-but-not-built" findings are now resolved — /portfolio/sip and /portfolio/rebalance pages are DELETED (AI SIP feature cut), the AUTOPILOT_LIVE_TRADING=false hard-off was REMOVED (AutoPilot is now Pro+Elite live with paper/live per-user mode), and a momentum signals page was added (06-17). What survives as real tech debt: marketing copy oversells the model count (landing says "12 AI models" + "Five AI engines"; engines page comment still says "8 ML/DL engines" though the rendered page now shows 4 cards under neutral copy), the design-token palette in lib/tokens.ts does NOT match the "AI Trading OS" palette named in the redesign brief (it ships "palette B" teal-green #2BD9BC on bg #0B0E15, not emerald #00E6A7 on #080B12), and the model edge is honestly weak (Qlib realmoney_pass:false, gate Sharpe ~0.1). Engineering discipline is high: zero TODO/FIXME/HACK comments in app source, no-fallback model loading, enforced backtest gate, honest empty-states. Test coverage is broad (148 pytest files, 23 Playwright specs across anon/authed/mobile projects). The redesign can sit on a solid, real backend and a 33-component foundation library, but must reconcile the palette drift and decide which stale-doc narrative is canonical.

strengths: Exceptional code hygiene: ZERO TODO/FIXME/HACK/XXX comments across backend/, ml/, and frontend/app+components+lib — the 'app is already clean, no dead-code pile' memory holds up.

Backend is overwhelmingly real, not a Potemkin village: 330 route decorators / 41 modules, no-fallback discipline (503 model_not_ready instead of fake output), enforced out-of-sample backtest gate (422 gate_failed) as the single path to live trades, $20/mo LLM kill-switch persisted + enforced.

Centralized, consistent gating spine: one FEATURE_MATRIX (core/tiers.py), RequireFeature/RequireTier deps -> structured 402 upgrade payload, admins bypass — a redesign can trust tier state from the API rather than reinventing it.

33-component shadcn-style foundation library + canonical lib/tokens.ts with first-class dark+light variants — the redesign already has a real design substrate (a token-swap can re-theme the whole app).

Broad automated test coverage: 148 pytest files + 23 Playwright e2e specs spanning anon, authed, and mobile projects (public pages, auth redirects, retired-route redirects, sidebar, caps meters, autopilot kill-switch, onboarding) — gives the redesign a regression safety net.

Honest empty-states + brand-firewall intent already in the codebase (no-fabricated-numbers MIN_SAMPLE>=8, honest-empty on scrape failure, public engine names Alpha/Mood/Regime/AutoPilot in lib/engines.ts).

Dual-mode (Managed beginner vs Pro) is genuinely shipped: separate nav (NAV_ITEMS_MANAGED), UiModeContext, deterministic zero-LLM /api/managed/overview, and 4 plain-language managed cards — the IA the redesign must preserve already exists.

issues.title: Reference audit docs are partially STALE — do not treat as current ground truth
issues.detail: Both docs are dated 2026-06-05/06 but the branch (feat/mldl-4engine) has moved on. Three of the doc's four headline 'sold-but-not-built' findings are now resolved: (1) /portfolio/sip and /portfolio/rebalance directories no longer exist (AI SIP cut); (2) AUTOPILOT_LIVE_TRADING=false hard-off was removed 06-12 (autopilot/page.tsx:54) and AutoPilot is now live on Pro+Elite; (3) a momentum signals page was added 06-17. A redesign planned off the docs alone would rebuild/avoid features that have already changed. Re-baseline before sequencing.

issues.title: Design-token palette in code does NOT match the 'AI Trading OS' palette named in the redesign brief
issues.detail: The brief specifies bg #080B12, emerald-teal #00E6A7, electric-blue #3B82F6, purple #8B5CF6, gold #FFB547, bull #00FF9D, bear #FF5B7F. lib/tokens.ts actually ships 'palette B': main bg #0B0E15, primary #2BD9BC (teal-green not emerald), highlight/accent #2962FF (TradingView blue not #3B82F6), up #16C995, down #F23645, warning #F5A623 (not gold #FFB547), cyan #00E5FF. Purple #8B5CF6 matches. This is a fundamental reconciliation: either the brief palette is aspirational-and-unbuilt, or tokens.ts is the real target. Must be decided before any visual work; a token swap re-themes the whole app via globals.css CSS variables, so getting the token values right is the highest-leverage redesign decision.

issues.title: Marketing copy still oversells model/engine count (credibility bug persists on landing)
issues.detail: Landing (frontend/app/page.tsx) still states 'NSE-native · 12 AI models' (:72), 'Five AI engines — Forecast · Alpha · Gate · Regime · Mood' (:81), and 'Trusted by ... institutional desks' (:142). Reality per ground truth: 5 signal models, 4 public engines (lib/engines.ts), no institutional desks. The engines page comment at :19 still says '8 ML/DL engines' (rendered page now neutral). The user is told 12, then 5, then sees 4 — the single most fixable credibility problem, and the redesign of marketing surfaces should fix the count and the public-brand naming (Forecast/Gate are internal, not the Alpha/Mood/Regime/AutoPilot/Counterpoint public set).
