issues.title: Per-feature LLM caps defined but unenforced (only chat + doctor metered)
issues.detail: LLM_FEATURE_CAPS in core/tiers.py defines per-tier daily/monthly limits for strategy_gen, scanner_thesis, chart_vision, debate, fno_advisor — but code-grep shows only chat (copilot credit limiter) and portfolio_doctor (hand-rolled monthly quota) are actually enforced. The rest rely solely on tier feature-gating + the $20 global kill-switch. Low money-risk (free models) but the per-user ceilings shown are paper rules. Not a frontend blocker but relevant if the redesign surfaces usage meters for those features.

issues.title: Two build paths (Dockerfile vs nixpacks) with divergent dependency strategies
issues.detail: railway.toml uses NIXPACKS (nixpacks.toml: python312+gcc, skips TA-Lib C lib, pip -r requirements.txt, force-pins huggingface-hub). Dockerfile takes a different path: builds the full TA-Lib 0.6.4 C library from source. Both target python-3.12 (runtime.txt) and the same uvicorn start. Maintaining two build definitions risks drift (e.g. TA-Lib present in Docker, absent in Nixpacks). Confirm which is canonical for deploy; vercel.json drives the frontend (@vercel/next on frontend/package.json).

issues.title: Potential brand-firewall leaks: real model names in 6 frontend files
issues.detail: grep for FinBERT/TFT/LightGBM/Qlib/HMM/Alpha158/pytorch in frontend/app+components hits 6 files: admin/model-performance (admin-only, OK), models/page.tsx (PUBLIC), autopilot/page.tsx, discover/AiTopPicks.tsx, signals/ExplanationMarkdown.tsx, signals/ModelAccuracyCard.tsx. The public /models page and signal explanation components could surface internal model names to users, violating the Alpha/Mood/Regime/AutoPilot/Counterpoint brand firewall. Audit these during redesign.

issues.title: Stale comments contradicting locked decisions (cosmetic, but misleads engineers)
issues.detail: engines/page.tsx:19 comment says '8 ML/DL engines' (4 defined). Per the docs, autopilot service header and page still reference removed RL ('regime_hmm v20+' vs live v24). Non-functional but a redesigning engineer reading source gets wrong context. Cheap to fix during touch.

issues.title: Underlying model edge is weak — a UI/UX redesign cannot fix substance
issues.detail: Ground truth (verified in docs against model_versions): Qlib Alpha158 v8 qlib_realmoney_pass:false at IC~0.03; LGBM gate is_prod=false yet a required 0.30-weight voter at CV Sharpe~0.1/~40% acc; TFT served checkpoint differs from registry PROD v3 (framework mismatch, the 0.68 dir-acc belongs to an unloaded artifact); 'Mood/FinBERT' is an LLM at runtime not FinBERT. The redesign should avoid amplifying accuracy/performance claims the models don't support; lean on the honest-empty-state discipline already present.

redesign_implications: RE-BASELINE FIRST: do not plan the redesign off the two audit docs verbatim — they are ~2 weeks stale on this branch (sip/rebalance deleted, autopilot live-off removed, momentum page added, route count 298->330). Spend the first redesign task re-walking the 62 live page.tsx routes against the current nav.ts (Pro NAV_ITEMS + Managed NAV_ITEMS_MANAGED) to get a true surface map.

DECIDE THE PALETTE BEFORE ANY VISUAL WORK: the brief's 'AI Trading OS' palette (#080B12/#00E6A7/#00FF9D/#FF5B7F) is NOT what lib/tokens.ts ships (palette B teal-green #2BD9BC/#0B0E15/#16C995/#F23645). Pick one as canonical. Because foundation components consume tokens via Tailwind classes -> globals.css CSS variables, a clean token swap re-themes the whole app — this is the single highest-leverage, lowest-risk lever, but only if the token contract is honored (no inline hex; lint hook already blocks raw hex per memory).

REUSE, DON'T REBUILD, THE FOUNDATION: the 33-component foundation lib + tokens + both themes are real and good. Redesign should restyle these primitives (Button/Card/Tabs/DataTable/StatCard/Sparkline/UsageMeter/EmptyState/PageHeader) rather than introduce a parallel component system — that propagates the new look everywhere for free and keeps the Playwright e2e selectors valid.

BACKEND IS UNTOUCHED TERRITORY: per the redesign-preserves-features rule, treat the 41 route modules / 330 endpoints + FEATURE_MATRIX tier gating + 402 upgrade-modal pattern as fixed contracts. Map every redesigned page back to its existing API call (api.ts is the typed client). Tier state, caps, and kill-switches all come from the API — the redesign consumes, never reimplements, gating.

PRESERVE DUAL-MODE IA: the header-less desktop shell (Topbar mobile-only), Settings-only theme toggle, and Managed-vs-Pro nav split are shipped decisions encoded in nav.ts/NavList/UiModeContext/AppShell. The redesign must carry both navs and the /home (managed) vs /dashboard (pro) split, not collapse them.

FIX THE NUMBERS NARRATIVE AS PART OF MARKETING REDESIGN: reconcile 12/8/5/4 model-count copy to one honest number (4 public engines / 5 signal models) and purge internal model names (Forecast/Gate/TFT/FinBERT/Qlib/HMM) from public surfaces (landing, /models, signal explanation components) to honor the brand firewall.

AUDIT PRICING vs REALITY: when redesigning /pricing, validate FeatureComparisonMatrix rows against the live FEATURE_MATRIX — remove or relabel AI SIP (deleted), F9 earnings (503/coming-soon), marketplace publish (deferred), and present WhatsApp honestly (dormant until BSP).

LEAN ON THE TEST NET: 23 Playwright specs (anon/authed/mobile, including route-walkthrough, sidebar, caps, kill-switch, onboarding) will catch redesign regressions — keep data-testid/role selectors stable through the restyle, and extend specs for any new redesigned surfaces.
